1	"""
2	HC-GRC main LangGraph graph — Phase 0 + Phase 1 exploratory subgraph.
3	
4	Graph topology (Phase 0 + Phase 1):
5	
6	  orchestrator
7	      ↓
8	  data_split          ← SHA-256 seeded 70/15/15 split (synthetic in Phase 0)
9	      ↓
10	  gate_1              ← interrupt() — pre-data checkpoint
11	      ↓ [approved]
12	  data_pipeline       ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
13	      ↓                  → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent
14	  exploratory_phase   ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential; TODO: Send() fan-out)
15	      ↓
16	  hypothesis_formalize ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
17	      ↓
18	  gate_2              ← interrupt() — SAP lock, hypothesis pre-registration
19	      ↓ [approved]
20	  confirmatory_entry  ← Phase 2 stub (route_after_gate_2 → "confirmatory_entry")
21	      ↓ [rejected]
22	  exploratory_phase   ← loop back to re-run EDA with revised hypotheses
23	
24	  gate_3, gate_4, gate_5 are Phase 2 mechanism work — their node functions live
25	  in nodes/gates.py but are NOT registered in this Phase 0/1 graph (they would be
26	  unreachable). They are wired into the confirmatory subgraph in Phase 2.
27	
28	Gate nodes use interrupt() to surface proposals to the operator.
29	All gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator (serialized, no concurrent writes).
30	
31	Per ADR-0015: run_id propagates through thread_config to PostgresSaver
32	and to all four observability stores.
33	"""
34	
35	from __future__ import annotations
36	
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph
40	from langgraph.graph.state import CompiledStateGraph
41	from langgraph.types import Command
42	
43	from .checkpointer import configured_checkpointer
44	from .infrastructure.observability.phoenix_setup import (
45	    bootstrap_observability,
46	    run_trace_context,
47	)
48	from .nodes.data_split import data_split_node
49	from .nodes.gates import gate_1_node, gate_2_node
50	from .nodes.orchestrator import route_after_gate_1, route_after_gate_2, t00_orchestrator_node
51	from .nodes.phase1 import (
52	    confirmatory_entry_node,
53	    data_pipeline_node,
54	    exploratory_phase_node,
55	    hypothesis_formalize_node,
56	)
57	from .state import HCGRCState, initial_state
58	
59	# Upper bound on graph super-steps per run. Bounds the Gate-2-rejected loop back
60	# to exploratory analysis so a non-converging run fails loudly (#179).
61	GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT = 50
62	
63	# Resilience is layered, NOT via node RetryPolicy (pass-4 #15): the reasoning_client
64	# retries transient LLM failures with backoff; the agent nodes isolate per-agent
65	# failures as a 'failed' status + failure_event (so one agent's error neither
66	# crashes the run nor is retried into a re-executed SAP violation); the checkpointer
67	# uses a self-healing pool; and GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT bounds loops. A node-level
68	# RetryPolicy was dead — the nodes catch their own exceptions before LangGraph
69	# would ever retry — and is therefore not used.
70	
71	# ── Graph builder ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
72	
73	def build_graph(checkpointer=None) -> CompiledStateGraph:
74	    """
75	    Build and compile the HC-GRC LangGraph graph.
76	
77	    Args:
78	        checkpointer: LangGraph checkpointer (MemorySaver for Phase 0/1,
79	                      PostgresSaver for production). If None, graph runs
80	                      without persistence (stateless — for unit tests only).
81	
82	    Returns:
83	        Compiled LangGraph graph.
84	    """
85	    builder = StateGraph(HCGRCState)
86	
87	    # ── Phase 0 nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
88	    builder.add_node("orchestrator", t00_orchestrator_node)
89	
90	    # data_split_node needs synthetic_control_ids in Phase 0.
91	    # In Phase 1, DataStewardAgent writes real splits to disk.
92	    # Phase 0 wrapper: synthetic IDs matching SCF scale for gate dry-runs.
93	    def _data_split_phase0(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
94	        synthetic_ids = [f"SCF-CTRL-{i:04d}" for i in range(1400)]
95	        return data_split_node(state, synthetic_control_ids=synthetic_ids)
96	
97	    builder.add_node("data_split", _data_split_phase0)
98	    builder.add_node("gate_1", gate_1_node)
99	
100	    # ── Phase 1 nodes ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	    builder.add_node("data_pipeline", data_pipeline_node)
102	    builder.add_node("exploratory_phase", exploratory_phase_node)
103	    builder.add_node("hypothesis_formalize", hypothesis_formalize_node)
104	    builder.add_node("gate_2", gate_2_node)
105	
106	    # ── Phase 2 stub ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107	    # gate_3/4/5 are NOT registered here: they have no inbound edges in Phase 0/1
108	    # and would be dead/unreachable nodes (#213). They are wired into the
109	    # confirmatory subgraph in Phase 2. Their node functions live in nodes/gates.py.
110	    builder.add_node("confirmatory_entry", confirmatory_entry_node)
111	
112	    # ── Edges — Phase 0 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
113	    builder.set_entry_point("orchestrator")
114	    builder.add_edge("orchestrator", "data_split")
115	    builder.add_edge("data_split", "gate_1")
116	
117	    builder.add_conditional_edges(
118	        "gate_1",
119	        route_after_gate_1,
120	        {
121	            "phase_1_entry": "data_pipeline",  # approved → Phase 1 data pipeline
122	            "end": END,
123	        },
124	    )
125	
126	    # ── Edges — Phase 1 exploratory ───────────────────────────────────────────
127	    builder.add_edge("data_pipeline", "exploratory_phase")
128	    builder.add_edge("exploratory_phase", "hypothesis_formalize")
129	    builder.add_edge("hypothesis_formalize", "gate_2")
130	
131	    builder.add_conditional_edges(
132	        "gate_2",
133	        route_after_gate_2,
134	        {
135	            "confirmatory_entry": "confirmatory_entry",  # approved → Phase 2
136	            "exploratory_entry": "exploratory_phase",    # rejected → re-run EDA
137	            "end": END,                                   # deferred → park
138	        },
139	    )
140	
141	    # ── Phase 2 stub edge ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
142	    # confirmatory_entry → gate_3 → gate_4 → gate_5 will be wired in Phase 2.
143	    # For now, confirmatory_entry routes to END so the graph compiles.
144	    builder.add_edge("confirmatory_entry", END)
145	
146	    # ── Compile ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147	    compile_kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {}
148	    if checkpointer is not None:
149	        compile_kwargs["checkpointer"] = checkpointer
150	
151	    return builder.compile(**compile_kwargs)
152	
153	
154	# ── Convenience runners ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
155	
156	def run_phase0_synthetic(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
157	    """
158	    Execute one complete Phase 0 synthetic run.
159	
160	    The graph will pause at Gate 1 and wait for operator input.
161	    This function is used in the governance dry-run.
162	
163	    For tests that need to skip the interrupt, use the graph's
164	    .invoke() with a pre-configured Command resume.
165	    """
166	    # interrupt() is a no-op without a checkpointer — the gate could not pause for
167	    # the operator. Default to the configured backend so the runner actually parks
168	    # at the gate (#200/#226).
169	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
170	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
171	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
172	    bootstrap_observability()
173	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
174	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
175	    # looping unbounded (#179).
176	    thread_config = {
177	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
178	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
179	    }
180	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
181	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
182	    return result
183	
184	
185	def resume_run(run_id: str, decision: str, rationale: str, checkpointer,
186	               *, gate_id: str | None = None, checkpoint_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
187	    """
188	    Resume a run parked at a gate interrupt with the operator's decision.
189	
190	    The runners invoke the graph once and return when a gate's interrupt() parks
191	    it; this is the other half of the loop (#162). The operator's decision is
192	    delivered via the ADR-0014 governance channel and injected here as the
193	    interrupt's resume value, so the gate node observes
194	    {'gate_id': ..., 'decision': ..., 'rationale': ...} and continues.
195	
196	    Pass gate_id (the gate the operator was deciding on) so the gate-correlation
197	    guard in _run_gate can reject a stale/misrouted decision targeted at a gate
198	    other than the one currently parked (pass-4 #3). Without it the guard is
199	    unreachable. The ADR-0014 channel should record and pass back the gate_id it
200	    surfaced.
201	
202	    Requires the SAME checkpointer the run was started with (thread_id == run_id).
203	    """
204	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
205	    bootstrap_observability()
206	    config: dict[str, Any] = {
207	        "configurable": {"thread_id": run_id},
208	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
209	    }
210	    if checkpoint_id:
211	        config["configurable"]["checkpoint_id"] = checkpoint_id
212	    resume_value: dict[str, Any] = {"decision": decision, "rationale": rationale}
213	    if gate_id is not None:
214	        resume_value["gate_id"] = gate_id
215	    with run_trace_context(run_id):
216	        return graph.invoke(Command(resume=resume_value), config=config)
217	
218	
219	def run_phase1_dry_run(run_id: str | None = None, checkpointer=None) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Execute a Phase 1 dry run through Gate 2.
222	
223	    Gate 1 and Gate 2 will pause for operator input. Data pipeline and P1-P5
224	    nodes will record stub_pending statuses (NotImplementedError caught). This
225	    verifies the Phase 1 graph topology before real SCF data is loaded.
226	    """
227	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
228	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
229	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
230	    bootstrap_observability()
231	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
232	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
233	    # looping unbounded (#179).
234	    thread_config = {
235	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
236	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
237	    }
238	    with run_trace_context(state["run_id"]):
239	        result = graph.invoke(state, config=thread_config)
240	    return result

1	"""
2	HCGRCState — canonical LangGraph state for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	All nodes read from and write to this TypedDict. Fields are append-only
5	where possible to preserve audit history within a run.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	import uuid
11	from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal
12	
13	from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
14	from typing_extensions import TypedDict
15	
16	# ── Phase labels ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
17	
18	Phase = Literal["phase_0", "phase_1", "phase_2"]
19	
20	# Gate identifiers 1–5
21	GateID = Literal["gate_1", "gate_2", "gate_3", "gate_4", "gate_5"]
22	
23	# Only these three decisions ever exist: the operator-response validator
24	# (_VALID_DECISIONS) rejects anything else, so the type reflects what can actually
25	# be written. ('pending' was never produced or routed on — removed pass-6.)
26	GateDecision = Literal["approved", "rejected", "deferred"]
27	
28	
29	# ── Reducers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	
31	
32	def _merge_artifacts(a: list[str], b: list[str]) -> list[str]:
33	    """Append b onto a, dropping any value already present in a OR earlier in b.
34	    Order-preserving, O(n+m). The previous `x not in set(a)` deduped against a but
35	    not within b, so a single agent returning a duplicate path slipped through —
36	    violating the 'deduped on merge' contract (pass-6)."""
37	    seen = set(a)
38	    out = list(a)
39	    for x in b:
40	        if x not in seen:
41	            seen.add(x)
42	            out.append(x)
43	    return out
44	
45	
46	def _merge_hypotheses(a: list[dict[str, Any]], b: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
47	    """Append b onto a, deduping by hypothesis 'id' (latest wins, position kept).
48	
49	    Entries without an 'id' are kept as-is. Prevents a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize
50	    loop from accumulating duplicate hypotheses into the pre-registered set."""
51	    merged = list(a)
52	    index = {h["id"]: i for i, h in enumerate(merged) if isinstance(h, dict) and h.get("id")}
53	    for h in b:
54	        hid = h.get("id") if isinstance(h, dict) else None
55	        if hid is not None and hid in index:
56	            merged[index[hid]] = h
57	        else:
58	            if hid is not None:
59	                index[hid] = len(merged)
60	            merged.append(h)
61	    return merged
62	
63	
64	# ── Core state ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
65	
66	
67	class GateStatus(TypedDict):
68	    """Written exclusively by GateCoordinatorNode to avoid concurrent writes."""
69	
70	    gate_id: GateID
71	    decision: GateDecision
72	    rationale: str
73	    reviewer: str  # "operator" | "claude" | "synthetic"
74	    timestamp_utc: str  # ISO-8601
75	    run_id: str
76	
77	
78	class HCGRCState(TypedDict):
79	    """
80	    Canonical platform state. run_id propagates to all four stores:
81	      - MLflow  (tags.run_id)
82	      - Phoenix (trace attribute run_id)
83	      - PROV-DM (activity IRI contains run_id)
84	      - PostgresSaver (thread_id column)
85	
86	    See RUNBOOK.md for cross-store query procedures.
87	    """
88	
89	    # ── Identity ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	    run_id: str  # UUID4, set at graph entry, propagated everywhere
91	    thread_id: str  # LangGraph thread ID (== run_id for single-run threads)
92	    phase: Phase
93	
94	    # ── Gate tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
95	    # Merge reducer: {**a, **b} ensures concurrent gate writes are safe by
96	    # construction. gate_coordinator_node is the canonical writer, but the
97	    # reducer protects against any future path that returns a partial update.
98	    # Fixes: ADR-0015 §75 serial guarantee (F1) + silent overwrite risk (F4).
99	    gate_status: Annotated[dict[GateID, GateStatus], lambda a, b: {**a, **b}]
100	
101	    # ── Failure events (written by gate nodes on rejection) ───────────────────
102	    # Agent Evolution monitors this store for routing re-optimization signals.
103	    failure_events: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
104	
105	    # ── Research artifacts (append-only) ──────────────────────────────────────
106	    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
107	    findings: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
108	
109	    # ── Data split record ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110	    # Un-reduced (last-write-wins) scalars. Written by exactly one node per phase —
111	    # data_split_node in Phase 0, DataStewardAgent in Phase 1 — never concurrently,
112	    # so last-write-wins is safe by sequencing, not by a reducer (#212). Immutable
113	    # after Gate 1. If these ever become concurrently written, add a reducer.
114	    data_split_manifest_hash: str | None  # SHA-256 of the raw manifest file
115	    data_split_seed: int | None  # derived from manifest hash (int.from_bytes)
116	    data_split_verified: bool  # True after idempotency assertion passes
117	
118	    # ── Phase 1 execution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
119	    # phase_1_ready: True after data pipeline writes validated splits to disk.
120	    # exploratory_complete: True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub).
121	    phase_1_ready: bool
122	    exploratory_complete: bool
123	
124	    # ── Data pipeline provenance ──────────────────────────────────────────────
125	    # Written by EmbeddingAgent after embedding run completes. Required at Gate 2
126	    # to confirm which model produced the embeddings used in exploratory analysis.
127	    # Keys: model_name, model_hash, corpus_dvc_hash, timestamp_utc
128	    embedding_manifest: dict[str, Any] | None
129	
130	    # ── Exploratory analysis artifacts (append, deduped) ──────────────────────
131	    # eda_artifacts: file paths to all EXP_* outputs from P1-P5 nodes.
132	    # Each node appends its artifact paths. Gate 3 verifies all 5 agents present.
133	    # Deduped on merge so a Gate-2 reject → re-run-exploratory loop cannot
134	    # accumulate the same artifact path repeatedly (#235). Membership tested against
135	    # a set so the merge is O(n+m), not O(n*m), as the artifact list grows (pass-5).
136	    # eda_agent_statuses: per-agent completion records.
137	    # Keys: agent_id, status ("completed"|"stub_pending"|"failed"), note, timestamp_utc
138	    eda_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], _merge_artifacts]
139	    eda_agent_statuses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
140	
141	    # ── Formalized hypothesis set (append, deduped by id) ─────────────────────
142	    # Written by HypothesisFormalizerAgent before Gate 2.
143	    # Each entry is a FormalHypothesis dict (see src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer).
144	    # Deduped by hypothesis id (latest wins) so a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize loop
145	    # cannot accumulate duplicate hypotheses into the set Gate 2 locks (pass-4 #2).
146	    hypothesis_set: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: _merge_hypotheses(a, b)]
147	
148	    # ── Orchestrator run-start grounding ──────────────────────────────────────
149	    # Produced by t00_orchestrator_node via the reasoning_client (Tier 2). The
150	    # orchestrator reasons about the run *inside the graph* rather than the work
151	    # being done by hand outside the framework (ADR-0016 — the agency contract).
152	    # None when the reasoning backend was unavailable (graceful degradation).
153	    run_grounding: str | None
154	
155	    # ── Escalation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
156	    escalation_issue_number: int | None  # GitHub issue number if parked
157	    escalation_reason: str | None
158	
159	    # ── LLM messages (for agent traces) ──────────────────────────────────────
160	    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]
161	
162	    # ── Provenance ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
163	    prov_activities: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
164	
165	
166	def initial_state(run_id: str | None = None) -> HCGRCState:
167	    """Return a clean initial state for a new run."""
168	    rid = run_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
169	    return HCGRCState(
170	        run_id=rid,
171	        thread_id=rid,
172	        phase="phase_0",
173	        gate_status={},
174	        failure_events=[],
175	        hypotheses=[],
176	        findings=[],
177	        data_split_manifest_hash=None,
178	        data_split_seed=None,
179	        data_split_verified=False,
180	        phase_1_ready=False,
181	        exploratory_complete=False,
182	        embedding_manifest=None,
183	        eda_artifacts=[],
184	        eda_agent_statuses=[],
185	        hypothesis_set=[],
186	        run_grounding=None,
187	        escalation_issue_number=None,
188	        escalation_reason=None,
189	        messages=[],
190	        prov_activities=[],
191	    )

1	"""
2	PostgresSaver configuration for HC-GRC.
3	
4	LangGraph uses the checkpointer to persist graph state between turns,
5	enabling interrupt/resume across the operator approval gates.
6	
7	Per ADR-0015 (#79): run_id propagates to PostgresSaver as thread_id.
8	Cross-store query procedure in RUNBOOK.md §5.
9	
10	LOCAL-FIRST CONSTRAINT: PostgreSQL instance runs on-device.
11	No state leaves the machine. SaaS checkpointers are disqualified.
12	
13	Connection config:
14	  Database: hcgrc
15	  Host: localhost (default)
16	  Port: 5432 (default)
17	
18	For Phase 0 testing: use MemorySaver (no Postgres required).
19	For Phase 1+: use PostgresSaver with the connection string below.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import atexit
25	import os
26	import threading
27	
28	from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver
29	
30	# Arbitrary fixed key for the Postgres session advisory lock that serializes
31	# checkpoint schema migrations across concurrent initializers (get_checkpointer).
32	_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY = 0x4843_4752  # "HCGR"
33	
34	# Guards the process-local one-time-init bookkeeping below. The PG advisory lock
35	# serializes the DDL across PROCESSES, but the in-memory check-and-set guards
36	# (_atexit_registered, _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS) are not atomic on their own — two
37	# threads calling get_checkpointer() concurrently could both pass the check and
38	# double-register the atexit handler / double-run setup(). This lock makes those
39	# check-and-set sequences atomic within the process (pass-5).
40	_init_lock = threading.Lock()
41	
42	# Pools opened by get_checkpointer, closed once at process exit. A single atexit
43	# handler closes them all — registering one per call would accumulate handlers
44	# across repeated get_checkpointer() calls (#249).
45	_OPEN_POOLS: list = []
46	_atexit_registered = False
47	
48	# conn_strs whose schema bootstrap (advisory lock + setup() DDL + migration SELECT)
49	# has already run in this process. setup() is idempotent but not free — re-running
50	# it on every get_checkpointer() call takes a global advisory lock and replays the
51	# migration version query at each run start. Bootstrap once per process per schema;
52	# the advisory lock still guards the first-time cross-process migration race (#276).
53	_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS: set[str] = set()
54	
55	
56	def _safe_close(pool) -> None:
57	    """Close one pool, swallowing errors so a single failing close() does not abort
58	    cleanup of the remaining pools at interpreter exit (pass-6). close() ultimately
59	    calls a C finish() that can raise."""
60	    try:
61	        pool.close()
62	    except Exception:
63	        pass
64	
65	
66	def _register_pool_cleanup(pool) -> None:
67	    global _atexit_registered
68	    with _init_lock:
69	        _OPEN_POOLS.append(pool)
70	        if not _atexit_registered:
71	            atexit.register(lambda: [_safe_close(p) for p in _OPEN_POOLS])
72	            _atexit_registered = True
73	
74	# ── Connection string ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	def get_postgres_connection_string() -> str:
77	    """
78	    Return the PostgreSQL connection string.
79	
80	    Precedence: HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL env var → the checkpointing.{host,port,database}
81	    section of platform.yaml → localhost default. This makes the platform.yaml
82	    checkpointing config live instead of dead (#225). Never logged, never committed.
83	
84	    ADR-0002: the resolved URI MUST point at a loopback host — graph state holds the
85	    full corpus analysis / hypotheses / gate decisions and must not leave the box.
86	    The env-var path bypasses config-load validation, so it is guarded here too
87	    (point-of-use defense-in-depth, mirroring Qdrant/MLflow) (pass-6).
88	    """
89	    from .infrastructure.config import require_local_uri
90	
91	    env_url = os.environ.get("HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL")
92	    if env_url:
93	        return require_local_uri(env_url, "checkpointing (HCGRC_POSTGRES_URL)")
94	    try:
95	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
96	        cp = load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}
97	        host = cp.get("host", "localhost")
98	        port = cp.get("port", 5432)
99	        database = cp.get("database", "hcgrc")
100	        url = f"postgresql://{host}:{port}/{database}"
101	    except Exception:
102	        return "postgresql://localhost:5432/hcgrc"
103	    # Validate OUTSIDE the try so a non-loopback host raises rather than being
104	    # masked by the localhost fallback.
105	    return require_local_uri(url, "checkpointing")
106	
107	
108	def configured_checkpointer():
109	    """
110	    Return the checkpointer the platform config asks for: MemorySaver when
111	    checkpointing.backend == 'memory', else the pooled PostgresSaver (#226).
112	    Runners use this as the default instead of hardcoding in-memory.
113	    """
114	    backend = "postgres"
115	    try:
116	        from .infrastructure.config import load_platform_config
117	        backend = (load_platform_config().get("checkpointing", {}) or {}).get("backend", "postgres")
118	    except Exception:
119	        backend = "postgres"
120	    return get_checkpointer(use_memory=(backend == "memory"))
121	
122	
123	# ── Checkpointer factory ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
124	
125	def get_checkpointer(use_memory: bool = False):
126	    """
127	    Return a configured checkpointer.
128	
129	    Args:
130	        use_memory: If True, return MemorySaver (Phase 0 testing, no Postgres needed).
131	                    If False, return PostgresSaver (Phase 1+ production).
132	
133	    Returns:
134	        MemorySaver | PostgresSaver
135	    """
136	    if use_memory:
137	        return MemorySaver()
138	
139	    try:
140	        from langgraph.checkpoint.postgres import PostgresSaver
141	        from psycopg.rows import dict_row
142	        from psycopg_pool import ConnectionPool
143	    except ImportError as e:
144	        raise ImportError(
145	            "langgraph-checkpoint-postgres and psycopg[pool] are required for PostgresSaver. "
146	            "Run: pip install 'langgraph-checkpoint-postgres' 'psycopg[binary,pool]'"
147	        ) from e
148	
149	    conn_str = get_postgres_connection_string()
150	    try:
151	        # A single raw Connection has no recovery: when the Postgres backend goes
152	        # away (node sleep/restart, idle timeout, network blip) the cached
153	        # connection is permanently broken and every subsequent checkpoint op
154	        # raises — silent loss of durability exactly during interrupt/resume across
155	        # the approval gates. Use a self-healing ConnectionPool instead: it runs a
156	        # liveness check on checkout and discards/replaces dead connections.
157	        # PostgresSaver natively accepts a ConnectionPool. (Hardening pass 1, #4.)
158	        pool = ConnectionPool(
159	            conn_str,
160	            min_size=1,
161	            max_size=8,
162	            kwargs={
163	                "autocommit": True,
164	                "prepare_threshold": 0,
165	                "row_factory": dict_row,
166	            },
167	            check=ConnectionPool.check_connection,
168	            open=True,
169	        )
170	        # PostgresSaver does not own the pool, so close it cleanly at process exit
171	        # (otherwise the pool's worker thread cannot be joined during interpreter
172	        # finalization, raising a noisy PythonFinalizationError). A single shared
173	        # atexit handler closes all pools — no per-call accumulation (#249).
174	        _register_pool_cleanup(pool)
175	        # Serialize schema migrations across processes with a session advisory
176	        # lock (pass-1 #33). The lock and the migration DDL MUST run on the SAME
177	        # connection — pg_advisory_lock is session-scoped, and a pool-backed
178	        # PostgresSaver.setup() would check out a *different* connection for the
179	        # DDL, leaving the lock ineffective (pass-3 #2). So run setup() through a
180	        # saver bound to the single locked connection, then return the pool-backed
181	        # saver for normal operation.
182	        # Bootstrap the schema once per process per conn_str. setup() is idempotent
183	        # but a redundant advisory lock + migration SELECT on every run start is
184	        # wasteful; the lock still protects the first-time cross-process race (#276).
185	        # _init_lock makes the in-process check-and-set atomic so concurrent threads
186	        # don't both run setup() (pass-5); the held lock also serializes the DDL,
187	        # which is fine — it runs at most once per conn_str per process.
188	        with _init_lock:
189	            if conn_str not in _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS:
190	                with pool.connection() as conn:
191	                    conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_lock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
192	                    try:
193	                        PostgresSaver(conn).setup()  # DDL on the locked connection
194	                    finally:
195	                        conn.execute("SELECT pg_advisory_unlock(%s)", (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY,))
196	                _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS.add(conn_str)
197	        return PostgresSaver(pool)
198	    except Exception as e:
199	        raise RuntimeError(
200	            f"PostgresSaver connection failed. Is Postgres running on {conn_str}? "
201	            f"Error: {e}\n"
202	            "For Phase 0 testing without Postgres, use get_checkpointer(use_memory=True)."
203	        ) from e
204

1	"""
2	Gate nodes for the HC-GRC research platform.
3	
4	Five gates enforce human approval at structural transition points.
5	Each gate issues a LangGraph interrupt(), surfacing a structured proposal
6	to the operator. The operator responds via Claude on any device (ADR-0014).
7	
8	Gate decisions are written by gate_coordinator_node — never directly here.
9	On rejection, each gate appends a failure_event for Agent Evolution monitoring.
10	
11	Gate definitions:
12	  Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint (Phase 0 complete, all prerequisites verified)
13	  Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory (hypothesis lock, test data unlock)
14	  Gate 3 — Finding significance review (before publication pipeline)
15	  Gate 4 — Manuscript submission approval
16	  Gate 5 — Escalation resolution gate (triggered by ADR-0014 Escalation loop)
17	"""
18	
19	from __future__ import annotations
20	
21	import datetime
22	from typing import Any
23	
24	from langgraph.types import interrupt
25	
26	from ..state import HCGRCState
27	
28	# ── Shared helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
29	
30	
31	def _build_gate_proposal(gate_id: str, title: str, summary: str,
32	                          checklist: list[str], run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
33	    """Build the structured proposal surfaced to the operator via interrupt()."""
34	    return {
35	        "gate_id": gate_id,
36	        "title": title,
37	        "summary": summary,
38	        "checklist": checklist,
39	        "run_id": run_id,
40	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
41	        "instructions": (
42	            "Respond with: {'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', "
43	            "'rationale': '<your reasoning>'}"
44	        ),
45	    }
46	
47	
48	def _rejection_event(gate_id: str, rationale: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
49	    """Failure event appended to state for Agent Evolution monitoring."""
50	    return {
51	        "event_type": "gate_rejection",
52	        "gate_id": gate_id,
53	        "rationale": rationale,
54	        "run_id": run_id,
55	        "timestamp_utc": datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat(),
56	    }
57	
58	
59	_VALID_DECISIONS = {"approved", "rejected", "deferred"}
60	# Only 'approved' is terminal — it stops the gate re-firing on re-entry (don't
61	# re-unlock). 'rejected' routes back for re-review and 'deferred' parks the run for
62	# the operator to re-trigger later; BOTH must re-fire the interrupt when the gate
63	# is re-entered, so neither is terminal (pass-3 #5).
64	_TERMINAL_DECISIONS = {"approved"}
65	
66	
67	def _parse_operator_response(resp: Any, gate_id: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
68	    """
69	    Validate the operator's interrupt() response and return (decision, rationale).
70	
71	    A malformed response must NOT be silently coerced to 'rejected' (that would
72	    let a non-dict or a typo'd decision masquerade as a real governance decision,
73	    #163/#180). Fail loud so the operator re-submits a well-formed response on the
74	    next resume.
75	    """
76	    if not isinstance(resp, dict):
77	        raise ValueError(
78	            f"{gate_id}: operator response must be a dict like "
79	            f"{{'decision': 'approved'|'rejected'|'deferred', 'rationale': '...'}}, "
80	            f"got {type(resp).__name__}."
81	        )
82	    decision = resp.get("decision")
83	    if decision not in _VALID_DECISIONS:
84	        raise ValueError(
85	            f"{gate_id}: operator response 'decision' must be one of "
86	            f"{sorted(_VALID_DECISIONS)}, got {decision!r}."
87	        )
88	    rationale = resp.get("rationale") or "No rationale provided."
89	    return decision, rationale
90	
91	
92	def _already_decided(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str) -> bool:
93	    """
94	    True if this gate already holds a TERMINAL decision (approved only).
95	
96	    Guards against a re-invocation re-firing the interrupt and re-running
97	    downstream effects on top of a terminal decision (#177). A 'rejected' record
98	    is deliberately NOT terminal: the Gate-2 reject → revise → re-review loop
99	    (route_after_gate_2 → exploratory → … → gate_2) must re-fire the interrupt so
100	    the operator is re-prompted, otherwise the gate short-circuits to {} and the
101	    graph spins until GraphRecursionError (pass-2 #184/#187). The loop-back path
102	    is responsible for producing the revised state that warrants re-review.
103	    """
104	    record = state.get("gate_status", {}).get(gate_id)
105	    return bool(record) and record.get("decision") in _TERMINAL_DECISIONS
106	
107	
108	def _finalize_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, decision: str,
109	                   rationale: str, reviewer: str = "operator") -> dict[str, Any]:
110	    """Build the gate node's state update: failure event (if not approved) + the
111	    authoritative gate_status record (written only by gate_coordinator_node)."""
112	    update: dict[str, Any] = {}
113	    if decision == "rejected":
114	        # 'deferred' is a park, not a failure — only a rejection is a failure_event
115	        # (a deferred decision recorded as a rejection mislabels the audit trail).
116	        update["failure_events"] = [_rejection_event(gate_id, rationale, state["run_id"])]
117	    from .gate_coordinator import gate_coordinator_node
118	    update.update(gate_coordinator_node(state, gate_id, decision, rationale, reviewer=reviewer))
119	    return update
120	
121	
122	def _run_gate(state: HCGRCState, gate_id: str, proposal: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
123	    """
124	    Drive one gate: idempotency guard, then interrupt() the operator and finalize.
125	
126	    Validation runs INSIDE a re-prompt loop (pass-3 #3): interrupt() consumes and
127	    persists the resume value before validation, so raising on a malformed value
128	    would strand it in the checkpoint and replay it forever. Instead, a malformed
129	    or mis-correlated response re-fires a fresh interrupt (next resume index)
130	    carrying the error, so the operator can re-submit and actually recover.
131	
132	    Gate-id correlation (pass-3 #4): if the operator response names a gate, it must
133	    match this gate — a stale/misrouted decision cannot be applied to the wrong gate.
134	    """
135	    if _already_decided(state, gate_id):
136	        return {}
137	    current = proposal
138	    while True:
139	        resp = interrupt(current)
140	        if isinstance(resp, dict) and resp.get("gate_id") not in (None, gate_id):
141	            current = {**proposal, "error": (
142	                f"response gate_id {resp.get('gate_id')!r} does not match {gate_id}")}
143	            continue
144	        try:
145	            decision, rationale = _parse_operator_response(resp, gate_id)
146	        except ValueError as exc:
147	            current = {**proposal, "error": str(exc)}
148	            continue
149	        return _finalize_gate(state, gate_id, decision, rationale)
150	
151	
152	# ── Gate 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	
154	
155	def gate_1_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
156	    """
157	    Gate 1 — Pre-data checkpoint.
158	
159	    Fires before any SCF data enters the system. The operator verifies that
160	    all Phase 0 prerequisites are complete (ADR-0015, #71):
161	
162	      1. LangGraph skeleton running
163	      2. Gate 1 fires with synthetic payload (this test)
164	      3. Data split idempotency verified (SHA-256 seed reproducible)
165	      4. PostgresSaver benchmark complete (throughput on target hardware)
166	      5. Governance dry-run complete (full escalation loop exercised)
167	      6. Diversity anchor models identified (T-01, ADR-0013)
168	
169	    The interrupt() surfaces the proposal and parks the graph until the
170	    operator responds. No SCF data is loaded before this gate approves.
171	    """
172	    checklist = [
173	        "LangGraph skeleton compiles and graph executes on synthetic payload",
174	        "Gate 1 interrupt fires and resolves correctly",
175	        "compute_data_split() produces identical splits on two independent runs (SHA-256 seed)",
176	        "PostgresSaver write/read benchmark complete — throughput logged to MLflow",
177	        "Governance dry-run complete — escalation loop exercised end-to-end",
178	        # ── Preregistration Ledger entries (logged before Phase 1 data ingestion) ──
179	        "LEDGER-0002: Diversity anchor model candidates logged (T-01 — 3 general + 2 anchors)",
180	        "LEDGER-0003: Fleiss kappa path selection logged (path 1: recruit raters | path 2: acknowledge unestimated)",
181	        # ── NOTE: DIVERGENCE-01 is a Gate 2 prerequisite, NOT Gate 1 ──────────────
182	        # DIVERGENCE-01 (operationalization of model-STRM divergence) requires the
183	        # STRM label distribution from EDA — which has not run yet at Gate 1.
184	        # Decision framework is defined in SAP §10. Specific operationalization is
185	        # selected post-EDA, logged to Preregistration Ledger before Gate 2.
186	        # Confirmed: SAP §10 DIVERGENCE-01 decision framework understood and acknowledged",
187	    ]
188	
189	    proposal = _build_gate_proposal(
190	        gate_id="gate_1",
191	        title="Gate 1 — Pre-Data Checkpoint",
192	        summary=(
193	            "Platform has completed Phase 0 verification. All structural prerequisites "
194	            "are in place before SCF corpus data enters the system. "
195	            "Approve to unlock data ingestion and begin Phase 1."
196	        ),
197	        checklist=checklist,
198	        run_id=state["run_id"],
199	    )
200	
201	    # interrupt() parks the graph and surfaces proposal to operator.
202	    # The operator's response dict is the return value of interrupt().
203	    return _run_gate(state, "gate_1", proposal)
204	
205	
206	# ── Gate 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
207	
208	
209	def gate_2_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
210	    """
211	    Gate 2 — Exploratory → Confirmatory transition.
212	
213	    The most consequential gate. The operator reviews the pre-registered
214	    hypothesis set and approves unlocking the test split. Uses ADR-0014
215	    Escalation infrastructure (GitHub issue → Claude → decision record).
216	
217	    Hard prerequisites (checked before interrupt fires):
218	      - DIVERGENCE-01 operationalization logged to Preregistration Ledger
219	      - Primary embedding model designated and logged
220	      - Hypothesis set complete and committed to PREREGISTRATION_LEDGER.md
221	      - Data split idempotency verified (data_split_verified == True)
222	    """
223	    # Verify hard prerequisites before surfacing this irreversible gate. Gate 2
224	    # unlocks the test split, so the machine-checkable preconditions for a sound
225	    # exploratory→confirmatory transition are ALL enforced here, not just the data
226	    # split (pass-3 #1): exploratory analysis must be complete and a non-empty
227	    # pre-registered hypothesis set must exist before the operator can even be asked.
228	    hard_failures = []
229	    if not state.get("data_split_verified"):
230	        hard_failures.append("data_split_verified is False — run compute_data_split() first")
231	    if not state.get("exploratory_complete"):
232	        hard_failures.append(
233	            "exploratory_complete is False — P1-P5 exploratory analysis has not finished")
234	    if not state.get("hypothesis_set"):
235	        hard_failures.append(
236	            "hypothesis_set is empty — no pre-registered hypotheses to lock at Gate 2")
237	
238	    if hard_failures:
239	        # Write an authoritative gate_status record so the router and operator see
240	        # the failure (#164/#178). Decision is DEFERRED, not rejected: a system
241	        # prerequisite failure must PARK the run (route_after_gate_2 deferred→END),
242	        # not feed it into the operator reject→revise→re-review loop, which would
243	        # spin forever because re-running exploratory cannot satisfy an unmet
244	        # prerequisite (pass-3 #20). The operator re-triggers once prereqs are met.
245	        rationale = "Hard prerequisite failure: " + "; ".join(hard_failures)
246	        # 'deferred' is non-terminal, so this path re-runs whenever gate_2 is
247	        # re-entered (operator re-trigger after the park, or the reject→exploratory
248	        # →…→gate_2 loop) while prereqs are still unmet. failure_events has a plain
249	        # append reducer (state.py:86), so re-appending an identical
250	        # gate_prerequisite_failure on every re-entry duplicates the audit record
251	        # and biases the Agent Evolution failure-count signal. Only append when this
252	        # exact deferred prerequisite failure is not already recorded (#267).
253	        prior = state.get("gate_status", {}).get("gate_2") or {}
254	        already_recorded = (

1	"""
2	T-00 Orchestrator — run-start grounding + Phase 0 routing.
3	
4	In Phase 0, T-00 routes the graph through:
5	  data_split → gate_1 → [approved: phase_1_entry | rejected: end]
6	
7	What is established here:
8	  - Run-start grounding: the orchestrator reasons about the run *through the
9	    framework* (reasoning_client, Tier 2) — see t00_orchestrator_node. This is
10	    the agency contract: orchestration happens inside the graph, not by hand
11	    outside it (ADR-0016 / MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md routes the orchestrator
12	    to Tier 2, escalating to Tier 3 only for hard decisions).
13	  - Routing function signature that all later phases extend
14	  - Gate decision inspection pattern
15	
16	Per ADR-0015 (#72): Agent Evolution monitors failure_events and
17	autonomously re-optimizes T-00 routing via DSPy between tiers.
18	Protected agents (P1-P5, statistical-analyst, hypothesis-formalizer)
19	cannot be modified by Agent Evolution without Escalation approval.
20	"""
21	
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	from typing import Any
25	
26	from ..reasoning_client import ReasoningError, Tier, complete
27	from ..state import HCGRCState
28	
29	# T-00's stakes tier (agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md): routine routing is
30	# local; only hard escalations invoke Tier 3.
31	_ORCHESTRATOR_TIER = Tier.T2
32	
33	_ORCHESTRATOR_SYSTEM = (
34	    "You are T-00, the orchestrator of the HC-GRC research platform — a "
35	    "LangGraph agency that empirically tests the Secure Controls Framework's "
36	    "STRM mappings under a five-gate human-in-the-loop governance topology. "
37	    "At run start you ground the run: state the immediate objective, the next "
38	    "structural step in the graph, the gate it leads to, and the preconditions "
39	    "that must hold before that gate can be approved. Be concise and concrete. "
40	    "You do not approve gates and you do not fabricate results — you orient the "
41	    "run. Respond in at most 120 words."
42	)
43	
44	# Static description of the graph ahead, fed to the orchestrator so its grounding
45	# is anchored to the real topology rather than invented.
46	_PHASE_TOPOLOGY = {
47	    "phase_0": (
48	        "Phase 0 (current): data_split (SHA-256 seeded 70/15/15) → Gate 1 "
49	        "(pre-data checkpoint). Gate 1 preconditions: LangGraph skeleton runs, "
50	        "data-split idempotency verified, PostgresSaver benchmark logged, "
51	        "governance dry-run complete. Approval unlocks Phase 1 data ingestion."
52	    ),
53	    "phase_1": (
54	        "Phase 1 (current): data_pipeline → exploratory P1-P5 → "
55	        "hypothesis_formalize → Gate 2 (exploratory→confirmatory; locks the "
56	        "pre-registered hypothesis set and unlocks the test split — irreversible)."
57	    ),
58	    "phase_2": (
59	        "Phase 2 (current): confirmatory subgraph → Gate 3/4/5. Test split is "
60	        "live only after Gate 2 approval; every confirmatory script carries a "
61	        "SAP header."
62	    ),
63	}
64	
65	
66	def route_after_gate_1(state: HCGRCState) -> str:
67	    """
68	    Conditional edge after Gate 1.
69	
70	    Returns the name of the next node based on the Gate 1 decision.
71	    LangGraph uses this string to select the next node in the graph.
72	    """
73	    gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
74	    gate_1 = gate_status.get("gate_1")
75	
76	    if gate_1 is None:
77	        # Gate 1 hasn't fired yet — shouldn't happen in normal flow
78	        return "end"
79	
80	    decision = gate_1.get("decision", "rejected")
81	
82	    if decision == "approved":
83	        return "phase_1_entry"
84	    elif decision == "deferred":
85	        # Platform parks; operator will re-trigger when ready
86	        return "end"
87	    else:
88	        # rejected — platform stays in Phase 0
89	        return "end"
90	
91	
92	def route_after_gate_2(state: HCGRCState) -> str:
93	    """Conditional edge after Gate 2."""
94	    gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
95	    gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2")
96	
97	    if gate_2 is None:
98	        return "end"
99	
100	    decision = gate_2.get("decision", "rejected")
101	
102	    if decision == "approved":
103	        return "confirmatory_entry"
104	    elif decision == "deferred":
105	        return "end"
106	    else:
107	        return "exploratory_entry"  # rejected: return to exploratory
108	
109	
110	def t00_orchestrator_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
111	    """
112	    T-00 orchestrator node — run-start grounding through the framework.
113	
114	    The orchestrator reasons about the run via the reasoning_client (Tier 2):
115	    it produces a concise grounding (objective, next step, the gate ahead, the
116	    preconditions) that is written to state["run_grounding"] and recorded in
117	    provenance. This is the agency contract — orchestration is performed by the
118	    graph, not by a human operating outside it.
119	
120	    Degrades gracefully: if the Tier 2 backend is unavailable (offline CI,
121	    dry-run with no Ollama), run_grounding stays None and the run proceeds. The
122	    graph never depends on a live model to advance.
123	
124	    Does not modify gate_status (only gate_coordinator_node may do that).
125	    """
126	    run_id = state.get("run_id")
127	    phase = state.get("phase", "phase_0")
128	    topology = _PHASE_TOPOLOGY.get(phase, _PHASE_TOPOLOGY["phase_0"])
129	
130	    prompt = (
131	        f"Run {run_id} is starting in {phase}.\n"
132	        f"Graph ahead: {topology}\n\n"
133	        "Ground this run."
134	    )
135	
136	    grounding: str | None = None
137	    try:
138	        grounding = complete(
139	            _ORCHESTRATOR_TIER, prompt, system=_ORCHESTRATOR_SYSTEM,
140	            run_id=run_id, agent_id="orchestrator",
141	        )
142	        note = f"T-00 run-start grounding produced via reasoning_client ({_ORCHESTRATOR_TIER.value})"
143	    except ReasoningError as exc:
144	        # Backend unavailable — record the degradation and proceed.
145	        note = f"T-00 grounding unavailable ({exc}); proceeding without grounding"
146	
147	    return {
148	        "run_grounding": grounding,
149	        "prov_activities": [
150	            {
151	                "activity": "t00_orchestration",
152	                "run_id": run_id,
153	                "phase": phase,
154	                "tier": _ORCHESTRATOR_TIER.value,
155	                "grounded": grounding is not None,
156	                "note": note,
157	            }
158	        ],
159	    }

1	"""
2	Phase 1 LangGraph node wrappers.
3	
4	Topology (Phase 1 exploratory subgraph):
5	
6	  phase_1_entry
7	      ↓
8	  data_pipeline          ← DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent
9	      ↓                    → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent (sequential)
10	  exploratory_phase      ← P1 → P2 → P3 → P4 → P5 (sequential now;
11	      ↓                    TODO: parallelize via Send() when implementations live)
12	  hypothesis_formalize   ← HypothesisFormalizerAgent
13	      ↓
14	  gate_2                 ← (in gates.py)
15	      ↓
16	  [approved: confirmatory_entry | rejected: exploratory_phase loop]
17	
18	Parallelization note:
19	  P1-P5 are currently called sequentially within exploratory_phase_node.
20	  When implementations are live, replace with Send()-based fan-out:
21	    def dispatch_exploratory(state) -> list[Send]:
22	        return [Send("p1_node", state), Send("p2_node", state), ...]
23	  Each worker appends to eda_artifacts and eda_agent_statuses via append reducers.
24	  After all workers complete, route to hypothesis_formalize.
25	  See LangGraph map-reduce pattern for the fan-in wiring.
26	
27	NotImplementedError handling:
28	  Each P1-P5 call is wrapped in try/except NotImplementedError. Agents that
29	  have not been implemented yet record a "stub_pending" status to eda_agent_statuses.
30	  This allows the graph to execute in tests and governance dry-runs without
31	  requiring all research implementations to be live.
32	"""
33	
34	from __future__ import annotations
35	
36	import datetime
37	from typing import Any
38	
39	from ..agents import (
40	    DataAcquisitionAgent,
41	    DataCurationAgent,
42	    DataStewardAgent,
43	    EmbeddingAgent,
44	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent,
45	    P1StrmNlpAgent,
46	    P2ControlTopologyAgent,
47	    P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent,
48	    P4RiskBlindspotAgent,
49	    P5AiGovernanceAgent,
50	)
51	from ..state import HCGRCState
52	
53	# ── Agent singletons ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
54	# Created once at module load; nodes call instance methods.
55	
56	_data_acquisition = DataAcquisitionAgent()
57	_data_curation = DataCurationAgent()
58	_data_steward = DataStewardAgent()
59	_embedding_agent = EmbeddingAgent()
60	
61	_p1 = P1StrmNlpAgent()
62	_p2 = P2ControlTopologyAgent()
63	_p3 = P3RegulatoryConvergenceAgent()
64	_p4 = P4RiskBlindspotAgent()
65	_p5 = P5AiGovernanceAgent()
66	_hypothesis_formalizer = HypothesisFormalizerAgent()
67	
68	
69	# ── Helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
70	
71	
72	def _utc_now() -> str:
73	    return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc).isoformat()
74	
75	
76	def _stub_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
77	    """Status record written when an agent has not been implemented yet."""
78	    return {
79	        "agent_id": agent_id,
80	        "status": "stub_pending",
81	        "note": (
82	            f"{agent_id} implementation pending — NotImplementedError caught. "
83	            "Will run when Phase 1 is live on Mac mini."
84	        ),
85	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
86	        "run_id": run_id,
87	    }
88	
89	
90	def _failed_status(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
91	    """Status record written when an agent raises a real runtime error (not a stub).
92	
93	    Produces the 'failed' status the state schema documents, so one agent's bug is
94	    recorded and isolated instead of crashing the whole subgraph (#148)."""
95	    return {
96	        "agent_id": agent_id,
97	        "status": "failed",
98	        "error": repr(exc),
99	        "note": f"{agent_id} raised {type(exc).__name__} during execution",
100	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
101	        "run_id": run_id,
102	    }
103	
104	
105	def _failure_event(agent_id: str, run_id: str, exc: BaseException) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	    """Failure event for Agent-Evolution / operator monitoring (append reducer)."""
107	    return {
108	        "event_type": "agent_runtime_error",
109	        "agent_id": agent_id,
110	        "error": repr(exc),
111	        "run_id": run_id,
112	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
113	    }
114	
115	
116	def _prov(activity: str, run_id: str, **kwargs: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
117	    """Minimal PROV-DM compatible activity record."""
118	    return {
119	        "activity": activity,
120	        "run_id": run_id,
121	        "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
122	        **kwargs,
123	    }
124	
125	
126	# ── Data pipeline node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	
129	def data_pipeline_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
130	    """
131	    Orchestrates the sequential data pipeline:
132	      DataAcquisitionAgent → DataCurationAgent → DataStewardAgent → EmbeddingAgent
133	
134	    Each stage is blocked on its predecessor completing successfully. If any stage
135	    raises NotImplementedError (Phase 1 stub), the pipeline records a stub_pending
136	    status and returns with phase_1_ready=False.
137	
138	    When all four stages complete: sets phase_1_ready=True in state.
139	    """
140	    run_id = state["run_id"]
141	    pipeline_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
142	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
143	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
144	
145	    stages = [
146	        (_data_acquisition, "data-acquisition"),
147	        (_data_curation, "data-curation"),
148	        (_data_steward, "data-steward"),
149	        (_embedding_agent, "embedding-agent"),
150	    ]
151	
152	    accumulated_update: dict[str, Any] = {}
153	
154	    for agent, agent_id in stages:
155	        try:
156	            stage_update = agent.run(state)
157	            pipeline_statuses.append({
158	                "agent_id": agent_id,
159	                "status": "completed",
160	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
161	                "run_id": run_id,
162	            })
163	            # Merge stage update into accumulated (agent writes prov_activities etc.)
164	            for k, v in stage_update.items():
165	                if k == "prov_activities" and isinstance(v, list):
166	                    prov.extend(v)
167	                else:
168	                    accumulated_update[k] = v
169	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
170	            # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError.
171	            # Once IMPLEMENTED, a NotImplementedError is a real bug, not a stub (#202).
172	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
173	                pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
174	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
175	                prov.append(_prov(
176	                    f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
177	                ))
178	                break
179	            pipeline_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
180	            # Cannot continue pipeline if a stage is not implemented
181	            # Phase 1 is not ready until all four stages complete
182	            prov.append(_prov(
183	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
184	                run_id,
185	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
186	            ))
187	            break
188	        except Exception as exc:
189	            # A real runtime error in one stage is recorded as 'failed' and halts
190	            # the pipeline (later stages depend on earlier ones) without crashing
191	            # the graph or discarding prior stages' output (#148). A governance
192	            # violation must propagate — it is not a recoverable pipeline fault.
193	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
194	            pipeline_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
195	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
196	            prov.append(_prov(
197	                f"data_pipeline_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
198	            ))
199	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
200	                raise
201	            break
202	
203	    # phase_1_ready only if all four stages completed (no stubs/failures in statuses)
204	    all_complete = all(s["status"] == "completed" for s in pipeline_statuses)
205	
206	    return {
207	        **accumulated_update,
208	        "phase_1_ready": all_complete,
209	        "eda_agent_statuses": pipeline_statuses,
210	        "failure_events": failures,
211	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("data_pipeline_node", run_id,
212	                                         all_complete=all_complete)],
213	    }
214	
215	
216	# ── Exploratory phase node ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
217	
218	
219	def exploratory_phase_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
220	    """
221	    Calls P1-P5 exploratory analysis sequentially. Catches NotImplementedError
222	    per agent and records stub_pending status.
223	
224	    PARALLELIZATION NOTE: When P1-P5 implementations are live, replace this node
225	    with a Send()-based fan-out. See module docstring for the pattern.
226	
227	    P1-P5 are PROTECTED agents — they are called via __call__() which dispatches
228	    to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]. Each returns a partial state
229	    update dict with eda_artifacts appended.
230	
231	    Gate 2 is NOT required here — this is exploratory phase. Each agent internally
232	    enforces the EXP_ artifact naming convention and no p-value decision language.
233	    """
234	    run_id = state["run_id"]
235	    agent_statuses: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
236	    all_eda_artifacts: list[str] = []
237	    failures: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
238	    prov: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
239	
240	    research_agents = [
241	        (_p1, "p1-strm-nlp"),
242	        (_p2, "p2-control-topology"),
243	        (_p3, "p3-regulatory-convergence"),
244	        (_p4, "p4-risk-blindspot"),
245	        (_p5, "p5-ai-governance"),
246	    ]
247	
248	    for agent, agent_id in research_agents:
249	        try:
250	            # __call__ dispatches to run_exploratory() based on state["phase"]
251	            agent_update = agent(state)
252	            artifacts = agent_update.get("eda_artifacts", [])
253	            all_eda_artifacts.extend(artifacts)
254	            agent_statuses.append({
255	                "agent_id": agent_id,
256	                "status": "completed",
257	                "artifact_count": len(artifacts),
258	                "timestamp_utc": _utc_now(),
259	                "run_id": run_id,
260	            })
261	            prov.append(_prov(f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}", run_id,
262	                               artifact_count=len(artifacts)))
263	        except NotImplementedError as exc:
264	            if getattr(agent, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
265	                # Real bug from an implemented agent — record as failed, not stub (#202).
266	                agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
267	                failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
268	                prov.append(_prov(
269	                    f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
270	                ))
271	                continue
272	            agent_statuses.append(_stub_status(agent_id, run_id))
273	            prov.append(_prov(
274	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_stub",
275	                run_id,
276	                note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 implementation pending",
277	            ))
278	        except Exception as exc:
279	            # P1-P5 are independent — one agent's runtime error is recorded as
280	            # 'failed' and the loop continues so the other agents' artifacts are
281	            # preserved, instead of crashing the whole exploratory subgraph (#148).
282	            # A governance violation must propagate, not be swallowed.
283	            from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
284	            if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
285	                raise
286	            agent_statuses.append(_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc))
287	            failures.append(_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc))
288	            prov.append(_prov(
289	                f"exploratory_{agent_id.replace('-', '_')}_failed", run_id, error=repr(exc),
290	            ))
291	
292	    all_stubs = all(s["status"] == "stub_pending" for s in agent_statuses)
293	    any_complete = any(s["status"] == "completed" for s in agent_statuses)
294	    exploratory_complete = any_complete and not all_stubs
295	
296	    # exploratory_complete = True only if at least one agent returned real results.
297	    # In Phase 1 dry-run (all stubs), stays False — Gate 2 checklist will surface this.
298	    return {
299	        "phase": "phase_1",
300	        "eda_artifacts": all_eda_artifacts,
301	        "eda_agent_statuses": agent_statuses,
302	        "failure_events": failures,
303	        "exploratory_complete": exploratory_complete,
304	        "prov_activities": prov + [_prov("exploratory_phase_node", run_id,
305	                                          exploratory_complete=exploratory_complete)],
306	    }
307	
308	
309	# ── Hypothesis formalization node ─────────────────────────────────────────────
310	
311	
312	def hypothesis_formalize_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
313	    """
314	    Calls HypothesisFormalizerAgent to produce the pre-registered hypothesis set.
315	
316	    Runs after exploratory analysis completes. Output populates state["hypothesis_set"]
317	    with FormalHypothesis dicts. This is the set that gets locked at Gate 2.
318	
319	    HypothesisFormalizerAgent is PROTECTED. Its run_exploratory() builds hypotheses
320	    from EDA outputs and HARKing validation stub. Output is the Gate 2 input.
321	    """
322	    run_id = state["run_id"]
323	    agent_id = "hypothesis-formalizer"
324	
325	    try:
326	        agent_update = _hypothesis_formalizer(state)
327	        hypothesis_count = len(agent_update.get("hypothesis_set", []))
328	        return {
329	            **agent_update,
330	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization", run_id,
331	                                       hypothesis_count=hypothesis_count)],
332	        }
333	    except NotImplementedError as exc:
334	        # Only an unimplemented agent legitimately raises NotImplementedError;
335	        # once IMPLEMENTED it is a real bug, not a stub (#237).
336	        if getattr(_hypothesis_formalizer, "IMPLEMENTED", False):
337	            return {
338	                "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
339	                "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
340	                "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
341	                                          error=repr(exc))],
342	            }

344	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_stub_status(agent_id, run_id)],
345	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_stub", run_id,
346	                                       note="NotImplementedError — Phase 1 pending")],
347	        }
348	    except Exception as exc:
349	        # Isolate a real runtime error: record it instead of crashing the graph,
350	        # and emit a failure_event for the monitor (#35). SAP violations propagate.
351	        from ..agents.base import SAPViolationError
352	        if isinstance(exc, SAPViolationError):
353	            raise
354	        return {
355	            "eda_agent_statuses": [_failed_status(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
356	            "failure_events": [_failure_event(agent_id, run_id, exc)],
357	            "prov_activities": [_prov("hypothesis_formalization_failed", run_id,
358	                                      error=repr(exc))],
359	        }
360	
361	
362	# ── Confirmatory entry stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
363	
364	
365	def confirmatory_entry_node(state: HCGRCState) -> dict[str, Any]:
366	    """
367	    Entry point for Phase 2 confirmatory analysis subgraph.
368	
369	    Gate 2 approval is required before this node runs (enforced by

371	    (statistical-analyst + P1-P5 confirmatory runs) is Phase 2 mechanism work.
372	    """
373	    return {

1	"""
2	reasoning_client — the ADR-0016 LLM execution-routing seam.
3	
4	A single abstraction that isolates LLM auth/transport from agent logic. Agents
5	declare *what* to reason about and at *what stakes tier*; this seam decides
6	*where* the call runs, how it authenticates, how it retries, times out, and how
7	it is traced. No agent imports an LLM client directly — that coupling lives here
8	and only here (ADR-0016 §"Implementation seam").
9	
10	Tiers (routing is by stakes, per ADR-0016 / agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md):
11	
12	    Tier.T1  — Deterministic ML. No LLM. complete() on T1 is a routing bug and
13	               raises: T1 work is computation, never inference.
14	    Tier.T2  — Local LLM on the Mac mini, 24/7 (Ollama). Bulk / low-stakes
15	               reasoning: per-pair classification, triage, drafting, review.
16	    Tier.T3  — Frontier Claude Max 20x via claude-agent-sdk. The `claude` CLI's
17	               subscription OAuth (CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) is the ONLY auth path
18	               — NO Anthropic API key. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is scrubbed from the SDK
19	               subprocess env so a stray key can never spill to the metered API
20	               (ADR-0016). Reserved for high-stakes judgments. Bursty / queued.
21	
22	Reliability (hardening pass 1):
23	  - Every call has a per-tier timeout (#145, #158) so a hung backend can never
24	    block a synchronous LangGraph node forever.
25	  - Transient failures are retried with bounded exponential backoff (#157).
26	  - Every call is wrapped in an OpenTelemetry span carrying run_id/agent_id/tier
27	    (OpenInference LLM conventions) so BOTH tiers are visible in Phoenix — T3 is
28	    not LangChain-instrumented, so without this it would be invisible (#150, #168).
29	    Spans are cheap no-ops when no tracer provider is registered (offline/tests).
30	  - complete_json uses Ollama's native JSON mode on T2 and a balanced-brace
31	    extractor, instead of a greedy regex that mangled multi-value replies (#159).
32	
33	Data sovereignty (ADR-0002 as amended by ADR-0016): persistent artifacts never
34	leave the machine; T3 permits transient, non-retained inference over the specific
35	SCF-derived text needed for a single call.
36	
37	Public surface:
38	    complete(tier, prompt, system=..., run_id=..., agent_id=...) -> str
39	    complete_json(tier, prompt, ...) -> dict | list
40	    is_available(tier) -> bool
41	"""
42	
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import json
47	import os
48	import threading
49	import time
50	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
51	from concurrent.futures import TimeoutError as FuturesTimeout
52	from enum import Enum
53	from functools import lru_cache
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	from opentelemetry import trace
57	
58	# ── Tiers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
59	
60	
61	class Tier(str, Enum):
62	    """Execution tier, by stakes. See agents/MODEL_TIER_ASSIGNMENTS.md."""
63	
64	    T1 = "t1"  # deterministic ML — no LLM
65	    T2 = "t2"  # local Ollama
66	    T3 = "t3"  # frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK
67	
68	
69	class ReasoningError(RuntimeError):
70	    """Raised when a reasoning call cannot be completed (backend down, timeout, T1 misuse)."""
71	
72	
73	class PermanentReasoningError(ReasoningError):
74	    """A non-transient failure (model missing, auth, CLI absent). Never retried."""
75	
76	
77	class RateLimitError(ReasoningError):
78	    """A Tier-3 subscription rate window. Retried with long backpressure, never
79	    spilled to a metered API (ADR-0016)."""
80	
81	
82	# ── Configuration (env-overridable; local-first defaults) ─────────────────────
83	
84	
85	def _env(name: str, default: str) -> str:
86	    val = os.environ.get(name, "").strip()
87	    return val or default
88	
89	
90	T2_MODEL = _env("HCGRC_T2_MODEL", "llama3.1:8b")
91	T2_BASE_URL = _env("HCGRC_OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")
92	T3_MODEL = os.environ.get("HCGRC_T3_MODEL", "").strip() or None
93	
94	# Per-tier hard timeouts (seconds). T3 is bursty/queued so it gets more headroom.
95	T2_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T2_TIMEOUT", "120"))
96	T3_TIMEOUT = float(_env("HCGRC_T3_TIMEOUT", "300"))
97	
98	# Tier-2 (Ollama) decoding controls. num_ctx avoids silent truncation at the 2048
99	# default (#196); seed makes greedy decoding reproducible (#197); num_predict caps
100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
