100	# output so a runaway/JSON-mode generation hits a bound before the hard timeout (#198).
101	T2_NUM_CTX = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_CTX", "8192"))
102	T2_SEED = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_SEED", "0"))
103	T2_NUM_PREDICT = int(_env("HCGRC_T2_NUM_PREDICT", "2048"))
104	
105	# Bounded retry with exponential backoff on transient failures. Clamped >= 0 so a
106	# negative override cannot produce an empty loop that raises None (#208).
107	MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_RETRIES", "2")))
108	BACKOFF_BASE = float(_env("HCGRC_REASONING_BACKOFF", "1.5"))
109	# Tier-3 rate-window backpressure: long, capped waits — queue and resume, never
110	# fail fast onto a metered path.
111	RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF = float(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_BACKOFF", "30"))
112	RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES = max(0, int(_env("HCGRC_RATELIMIT_RETRIES", "5")))
113	
114	# Truncation cap for prompt/response text recorded on spans (local Phoenix only).
115	_SPAN_TEXT_CAP = 8000
116	
117	_tracer = trace.get_tracer("hcgrc.reasoning_client")
118	
119	# Shared executor for enforcing sync-call timeouts. Not shut down: a hung backend
120	# call leaks one daemon worker rather than blocking the node (a ThreadPoolExecutor
121	# context-manager exit would join the hung thread, defeating the timeout).
122	_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS = 8
123	_timeout_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(
124	    max_workers=_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout"
125	)
126	
127	# In-flight worker accounting (#272). A timed-out call abandons (does NOT cancel)
128	# its worker — ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread blocked in
129	# llm.invoke/asyncio.run — so a truly-hung backend leaks that slot until (if ever)
130	# the call returns. Without a bound, accumulated leaks saturate the 8-slot pool and
131	# every subsequent submit silently queues behind work that never starts, converting
132	# ALL traffic into guaranteed timeouts. We track in-flight slots and fail FAST and
133	# LOUD with a distinct error when the pool is saturated, instead of degrading into a
134	# silent cliff. The per-tier inner timeouts (T2 HTTP timeout, T3 asyncio.wait_for)
135	# normally let workers self-terminate, so this guard only fires under genuine leaks.
136	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
137	_inflight = 0
138	
139	
140	def _submit_bounded(fn):
141	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if all worker slots are already in
142	    flight (i.e. leaked to hung backends). Decrements the count when the future
143	    completes — a leaked worker keeps its slot counted until it finally returns."""
144	    global _inflight
145	    with _inflight_lock:
146	        if _inflight >= _TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS:
147	            raise ReasoningError(
148	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_TIMEOUT_POOL_WORKERS} "
149	                "workers in-flight) — hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
150	            )
151	        _inflight += 1
152	
153	    def _release(_):
154	        global _inflight
155	        with _inflight_lock:
156	            _inflight -= 1
157	
158	    # Submit only after incrementing, but if submit() itself raises (e.g. the pool
159	    # was shut down — "cannot schedule new futures after shutdown") the done-callback
160	    # never fires, so decrement here to avoid permanently leaking the slot and
161	    # eventually false-saturating the pool (pass-5).
162	    try:
163	        future = _timeout_pool.submit(fn)
164	    except BaseException:
165	        with _inflight_lock:
166	            _inflight -= 1
167	        raise
168	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
169	    return future
170	
171	
172	# ── Timeout helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
173	
174	
175	def _with_timeout(fn, timeout: float, what: str):
176	    """Run a sync callable with a hard timeout; raise ReasoningError on overrun.
177	
178	    Propagates the current OpenTelemetry context into the worker thread so spans
179	    created by fn (e.g. the LangChain ChatOllama span) nest under the active
180	    reasoning.complete span instead of being orphaned (#238).
181	    """
182	    from opentelemetry import context as otel_context
183	
184	    ctx = otel_context.get_current()
185	
186	    def _run():
187	        token = otel_context.attach(ctx)
188	        try:
189	            return fn()
190	        finally:
191	            otel_context.detach(token)
192	
193	    future = _submit_bounded(_run)
194	    try:
195	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
196	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
197	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
198	
199	
200	def _run_sync(coro: Any) -> Any:
201	    """Run a coroutine to completion from sync code, even inside a running loop."""
202	    try:
203	        asyncio.get_running_loop()
204	    except RuntimeError:
205	        return asyncio.run(coro)
206	    # Bound the cross-thread wait so a starved/leaked pool can't deadlock a node
207	    # forever (#227). The inner coroutine already has its own asyncio timeout; this
208	    # is a backstop slightly beyond it. _submit_bounded fails fast if the pool is
209	    # already saturated with leaked workers rather than queueing forever (#272).
210	    return _submit_bounded(lambda: asyncio.run(coro)).result(timeout=T3_TIMEOUT + 30)
211	
212	
213	# ── Tier 2: local Ollama ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214	
215	
216	@lru_cache(maxsize=16)
217	def _ollama(model: str, temperature: float, json_mode: bool) -> Any:
218	    """Construct (and cache) a ChatOllama client for the local serving stack."""
219	    try:
220	        from langchain_ollama import ChatOllama
221	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
222	        raise ReasoningError(
223	            "langchain-ollama is not installed — Tier 2 backend unavailable."
224	        ) from exc
225	    kwargs: dict[str, Any] = {
226	        "model": model,
227	        "temperature": temperature,
228	        "base_url": T2_BASE_URL,
229	        "num_ctx": T2_NUM_CTX,         # avoid silent truncation at the 2048 default (#196)
230	        "seed": T2_SEED,               # reproducible greedy decoding (#197)
231	        "client_kwargs": {"timeout": T2_TIMEOUT},  # native HTTP timeout (#195)
232	    }
233	    if json_mode:
234	        # Native structured-output: Ollama constrains decoding to valid JSON,
235	        # far more reliable than instructing + regex-extracting (#159, #175). Do
236	        # NOT cap num_predict here — format=json terminates at the end of a valid
237	        # JSON value, and a num_predict cap would truncate it into INVALID JSON
238	        # (#231). The hard timeout remains the runaway backstop.
239	        kwargs["format"] = "json"
240	    else:
241	        kwargs["num_predict"] = T2_NUM_PREDICT  # bound free-text output length (#198)
242	    return ChatOllama(**kwargs)
243	
244	
245	def _t2_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float, model: str,
246	                 json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
247	    llm = _ollama(model, temperature, json_mode)
248	    messages: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
249	    if system:
250	        messages.append(("system", system))
251	    messages.append(("human", prompt))
252	
253	    def _call():
254	        resp = llm.invoke(messages)
255	        content = getattr(resp, "content", resp)
256	        text = content if isinstance(content, str) else str(content)
257	        return text, getattr(resp, "usage_metadata", None)  # token counts (#279)
258	
259	    try:
260	        # Give the outer future timeout margin over the inner HTTP timeout
261	        # (client_kwargs timeout == T2_TIMEOUT) so the inner socket timeout wins and
262	        # the worker self-terminates cleanly, instead of racing exactly and risking
263	        # an abandoned (leaked) worker — mirrors the T3_TIMEOUT + 30 backstop (#272).
264	        text, usage = _with_timeout(
265	            _call, T2_TIMEOUT + 10, f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL})"
266	        )
267	        text = text.strip()
268	        if not text:
269	            # An empty/whitespace reply is not a valid result — surface it as a
270	            # retryable error rather than silently returning "" and corrupting a
271	            # JSON caller or recording an empty high-stakes answer (#233).
272	            raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) returned an empty reply")
273	        return text, {"model": model, "usage": usage}
274	    except ReasoningError:
275	        raise
276	    except Exception as exc:
277	        msg = str(exc).lower()
278	        # A missing model is permanent — retrying just wastes backoff (#194).
279	        if "not found" in msg or "no such model" in msg or "try pulling" in msg:
280	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
281	                f"Tier 2 model {model!r} not available on Ollama: {exc}"
282	            ) from exc
283	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 2 (Ollama @ {T2_BASE_URL}) call failed: {exc}") from exc
284	
285	
286	# ── Tier 3: frontier Claude Max via Agent SDK ─────────────────────────────────
287	
288	
289	def _scrubbed_env() -> dict[str, str]:
290	    """
291	    Environment for the Agent SDK subprocess with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY removed.
292	
293	    ADR-0016: execution runs entirely on the fixed Claude Max subscription via the
294	    `claude` CLI's OAuth token. A metered Anthropic API key must NEVER be used — if
295	    one is present in the process env it would silently bill per-token, so we strip
296	    it before the SDK ever sees it.
297	    """
298	    return {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"}
299	
300	
301	async def _t3_async(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
302	                    model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
303	    try:
304	        from claude_agent_sdk import (
305	            AssistantMessage,
306	            ClaudeAgentOptions,
307	            ResultMessage,
308	            TextBlock,
309	            query,
310	        )
311	    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - dependency guard
312	        raise PermanentReasoningError(
313	            "claude-agent-sdk is not installed — Tier 3 backend unavailable."
314	        ) from exc
315	
316	    opts = ClaudeAgentOptions(
317	        max_turns=max_turns,
318	        system_prompt=system,
319	        allowed_tools=[],          # pure reasoning — no filesystem/command access
320	        env=_scrubbed_env(),       # ADR-0016: no metered API key (#146)
321	        setting_sources=[],        # don't load ANY on-disk settings into the
322	                                   # subprocess — keep the sandbox closed (#12)
323	        permission_mode="dontAsk",  # never wait on an interactive permission prompt
324	                                    # in this headless, single-turn, no-tools path —
325	                                    # deny if not pre-approved so any attempted tool
326	                                    # use fails fast instead of hanging until the
327	                                    # T3_TIMEOUT backstop (#274)
328	    )
329	    if model:
330	        opts.model = model
331	
332	    chunks: list[str] = []
333	    meta: dict[str, Any] = {"model": model}  # real model id if pinned, else unknown
334	    result_error: dict[str, Any] | None = None
335	
336	    async def _drain():
337	        nonlocal result_error
338	        async for msg in query(prompt=prompt, options=opts):
339	            # The SDK emits a first-class RateLimitEvent ahead of / instead of a
340	            # ResultMessage error. ONLY rate_limit_info.status == 'rejected' is a hard
341	            # rate window — 'allowed'/'allowed_warning' are informational and must NOT
342	            # trigger the 30s+ backpressure path (pass-6, SDK contract). Keep a generic
343	            # status=='rejected' fallback for other rejected-message shapes (#266).
344	            if type(msg).__name__ == "RateLimitEvent":
345	                rl_status = getattr(getattr(msg, "rate_limit_info", None), "status", None)
346	                if rl_status == "rejected":
347	                    result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["RateLimitEvent"],
348	                                    "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
349	                continue
350	            if getattr(msg, "status", None) == "rejected":
351	                result_error = {"status": 429, "errors": ["rejected"], "stop_reason": "rate_limit"}
352	                continue
353	            if isinstance(msg, AssistantMessage):
354	                if getattr(msg, "model", None):
355	                    meta["model"] = msg.model  # the actual model the run used (#204)
356	                for block in msg.content:
357	                    if isinstance(block, TextBlock):
358	                        chunks.append(block.text)
359	            elif isinstance(msg, ResultMessage):
360	                if getattr(msg, "usage", None):
361	                    meta["usage"] = msg.usage  # token usage for the span (#203)
362	                if getattr(msg, "total_cost_usd", None) is not None:
363	                    meta["cost_usd"] = msg.total_cost_usd
364	                # Capture the model's stop reason on SUCCESSFUL runs too (not just
365	                # errors) so the span records why generation ended — end_turn vs
366	                # max_tokens vs tool_use (OpenInference llm.finish_reason) (pass-5).
367	                if getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None):
368	                    meta["stop_reason"] = msg.stop_reason
369	                # The CLI reports API failures (incl. rate windows) by COMPLETING
370	                # the stream with is_error=True / api_error_status set, NOT by
371	                # raising. Capture it so a failed run is not returned as success
372	                # (pass-4 #1).
373	                if getattr(msg, "is_error", False):
374	                    result_error = {
375	                        "status": getattr(msg, "api_error_status", None),
376	                        "errors": getattr(msg, "errors", None),
377	                        "stop_reason": getattr(msg, "stop_reason", None),
378	                    }
379	
380	    await asyncio.wait_for(_drain(), timeout=T3_TIMEOUT)
381	
382	    if result_error is not None:
383	        status = result_error.get("status")
384	        detail = result_error.get("errors") or result_error.get("stop_reason") or status
385	        if status in (429, 529):
386	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window (api_error_status={status}): {detail}")
387	        if status in (401, 403):
388	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 auth failure ({status}): {detail}")
389	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 run reported an error (status={status}): {detail}")
390	
391	    return "".join(chunks).strip(), meta
392	
393	
394	def _t3_complete(prompt: str, system: str | None, max_turns: int,
395	                 model: str | None) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
396	    try:
397	        text, meta = _run_sync(_t3_async(prompt, system, max_turns, model))
398	        if not text:
399	            # An empty frontier reply is not a valid high-stakes result — retry

400	            # rather than return "" as success (#246).
401	            raise ReasoningError("Tier 3 (Agent SDK) returned an empty reply")
402	        return text, meta
403	    except ReasoningError:
404	        raise
405	    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, FuturesTimeout) as exc:
406	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) timed out after {T3_TIMEOUT:.0f}s") from exc
407	    except Exception as exc:
408	        # Classify on both the message and the exception type name — substring
409	        # matching alone is fragile across SDK versions (#229).
410	        sig = f"{type(exc).__name__} {exc}".lower()
411	        # Subscription rate window — queue and resume with long backpressure,
412	        # never spill to a metered API (ADR-0016, #185).
413	        if any(k in sig for k in ("rate limit", "rate_limit", "ratelimit", "429",
414	                                  "overloaded", "usage limit", "too many requests")):
415	            raise RateLimitError(f"Tier 3 rate window: {exc}") from exc
416	        # CLI absent / auth failure — permanent, not worth retrying (#194).
417	        if any(k in sig for k in ("not found", "notfound", "command not found",
418	                                  "no such file", "auth", "credential", "401",
419	                                  "403", "unauthor")):
420	            raise PermanentReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) unavailable: {exc}") from exc
421	        raise ReasoningError(f"Tier 3 (Agent SDK) call failed: {exc}") from exc
422

427	def _attempt(tier: Tier, prompt: str, system: str | None, temperature: float,
428	             max_turns: int, model: str | None, json_mode: bool) -> tuple[str, dict[str, Any]]:
429	    if tier is Tier.T2:
430	        return _t2_complete(prompt, system, temperature, model or T2_MODEL, json_mode)
431	    return _t3_complete(prompt, system, max_turns, model or T3_MODEL)
432

434	def _record_usage(span, usage: Any) -> None:
435	    """Set OpenInference token-count attributes from a usage dict/object (#203)."""
436	    if not usage:
437	        return
438	
439	    def g(key: str):
440	        return usage.get(key) if isinstance(usage, dict) else getattr(usage, key, None)
441	
442	    prompt_toks = g("input_tokens")
443	    completion_toks = g("output_tokens")
444	    # Anthropic/Agent-SDK report cache tokens in SEPARATE fields excluded from
445	    # input_tokens; on a cached-prefix run they can dominate real input. Fold them
446	    # into the prompt/total counts (and emit the OpenInference cache slots) so
447	    # llm.token_count.total reflects all tokens processed, not an undercount on the
448	    # very tier where usage visibility matters most (#280). Real spend is still
449	    # tracked separately via llm.cost_usd.
450	    cache_read = g("cache_read_input_tokens")
451	    cache_write = g("cache_creation_input_tokens")
452	    if cache_read is not None:
453	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_read", cache_read)
454	    if cache_write is not None:
455	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt_details.cache_write", cache_write)
456	
457	    prompt_total = None
458	    if prompt_toks is not None or cache_read is not None or cache_write is not None:
459	        prompt_total = (prompt_toks or 0) + (cache_read or 0) + (cache_write or 0)
460	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.prompt", prompt_total)
461	    if completion_toks is not None:
462	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.completion", completion_toks)
463	    if prompt_total is not None and completion_toks is not None:
464	        span.set_attribute("llm.token_count.total", prompt_total + completion_toks)
465

481	
482	    Times out per tier, retries transient failures with backoff, and emits an
483	    OpenTelemetry span (run_id/agent_id/tier) so the call is visible in Phoenix.
484	
485	    Raises ReasoningError on T1 misuse or after retries are exhausted. Callers
486	    that must keep the graph moving (dry-runs) should catch and degrade.
487	    """
488	    if tier is Tier.T1:
489	        raise ReasoningError(
490	            "Tier.T1 is deterministic ML — it has no LLM. A reasoning_client call "
491	            "on T1 is a routing bug; the work belongs in computation, not inference."
492	        )
493	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING", "").strip():
494	        raise ReasoningError("reasoning disabled via HCGRC_DISABLE_REASONING")
495	
496	    # Fall back to the run_id stamped in OTel baggage by run_trace_context, so a
497	    # seam span carries the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it (#239).
498	    if run_id is None:
499	        try:
500	            from opentelemetry import baggage
501	            run_id = baggage.get_baggage("hcgrc.run_id") or None
502	        except Exception:
503	            run_id = None
504	
505	    # Known model id only — never fabricate one (#204). For a T3 run on the
506	    # subscription default, the real model is read back from the response.
507	    known_model = model or (T2_MODEL if tier is Tier.T2 else T3_MODEL)
508	    with _tracer.start_as_current_span("reasoning.complete") as span:
509	        # For T2, LangChainInstrumentor emits a child ChatOllama span under this
510	        # one — intentional parent/child nesting (the seam-level call + the
511	        # provider call), not redundant double-tracing (#214). T3 has no LangChain
512	        # span, so this is the only record of the frontier call.
513	        # OpenInference LLM conventions + HC-GRC cross-store correlation.
514	        span.set_attribute("openinference.span.kind", "LLM")
515	        span.set_attribute("llm.provider", "ollama" if tier is Tier.T2 else "anthropic")
516	        if known_model:
517	            span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", known_model)
518	        span.set_attribute("hcgrc.tier", tier.value)
519	        if run_id:
520	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.run_id", run_id)
521	            span.set_attribute("session.id", run_id)
522	        if agent_id:
523	            span.set_attribute("hcgrc.agent_id", agent_id)
524	        if system:
525	            # Record the system prompt too — without it the trace input is
526	            # incomplete and the call is not reproducible from the span (#240).
527	            span.set_attribute("llm.system", system[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
528	        span.set_attribute("input.value", prompt[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
529	
530	        last_exc: ReasoningError | None = None
531	        # Independent retry budgets so a mix of transient and rate-limit errors
532	        # cannot exhaust each other's allowance prematurely (#228).
533	        transient_attempts = 0
534	        rate_attempts = 0
535	        while True:
536	            try:
537	                out, meta = _attempt(tier, prompt, system, temperature, max_turns, model, _json_mode)
538	                if meta.get("model"):
539	                    span.set_attribute("llm.model_name", meta["model"])
540	                _record_usage(span, meta.get("usage"))
541	                if meta.get("cost_usd") is not None:
542	                    span.set_attribute("llm.cost_usd", meta["cost_usd"])
543	                if meta.get("stop_reason"):
544	                    span.set_attribute("llm.finish_reason", meta["stop_reason"])  # (pass-5)
545	                span.set_attribute("output.value", out[:_SPAN_TEXT_CAP])
546	                span.set_attribute("llm.retries", transient_attempts + rate_attempts)
547	                return out
548	            except PermanentReasoningError as exc:
549	                # Deterministic failure — do not retry (#194).
550	                last_exc = exc
551	                break
552	            except RateLimitError as exc:
553	                last_exc = exc
554	                if rate_attempts >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES:
555	                    break
556	                sleep_s = RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF * (rate_attempts + 1)

570	        span.set_status(trace.StatusCode.ERROR, str(last_exc))

164	    .invoke() with a pre-configured Command resume.
165	    """

168	    # at the gate (#200/#226).
169	    checkpointer = checkpointer or configured_checkpointer()
170	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
171	    state = initial_state(run_id=run_id)
172	    bootstrap_observability()
173	    # Explicit recursion_limit bounds the Gate-2-rejected → exploratory loop so a
174	    # run that never converges terminates with GraphRecursionError instead of
175	    # looping unbounded (#179).

177	        "configurable": {"thread_id": state["run_id"]},
178	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,
179	    }

185	def resume_run(run_id: str, decision: str, rationale: str, checkpointer,
186	               *, gate_id: str | None = None, checkpoint_id: str | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
187	    """
188	    Resume a run parked at a gate interrupt with the operator's decision.
189	
190	    The runners invoke the graph once and return when a gate's interrupt() parks
191	    it; this is the other half of the loop (#162). The operator's decision is
192	    delivered via the ADR-0014 governance channel and injected here as the
193	    interrupt's resume value, so the gate node observes
194	    {'gate_id': ..., 'decision': ..., 'rationale': ...} and continues.
195	
196	    Pass gate_id (the gate the operator was deciding on) so the gate-correlation
197	    guard in _run_gate can reject a stale/misrouted decision targeted at a gate
198	    other than the one currently parked (pass-4 #3). Without it the guard is
199	    unreachable. The ADR-0014 channel should record and pass back the gate_id it
200	    surfaced.
201	
202	    Requires the SAME checkpointer the run was started with (thread_id == run_id).
203	    """
204	    graph = build_graph(checkpointer=checkpointer)
205	    bootstrap_observability()
206	    config: dict[str, Any] = {

208	        "recursion_limit": GRAPH_RECURSION_LIMIT,

213	    if gate_id is not None:
214	        resume_value["gate_id"] = gate_id
215	    with run_trace_context(run_id):

1	"""
2	Phoenix observability bootstrap for HC-GRC (roadmap Phase 0, deliverable #2).
3	
4	ADR-0009: Phoenix is the local, self-hosted LLM-tracing stack (LangSmith was
5	disqualified as SaaS-only). The Phoenix server runs on the compute node and
6	agent LLM calls export OpenTelemetry traces to it.
7	
8	Two responsibilities, deliberately separated:
9	
10	  1. The SERVER is a long-lived process — run it via the `phoenix serve` CLI
11	     (see launch_server_command()) or the launchd plist in scripts/. It is NOT
12	     launched from inside the application process.
13	
14	  2. INSTRUMENTATION is wired per-process via bootstrap_observability(), called
15	     once by each run entrypoint. It registers a global OTel tracer provider
16	     (BatchSpanProcessor — spans are buffered and exported in the background, so
17	     a momentary server outage drops spans rather than blocking or failing the
18	     run) pointed at the running server, and turns on the LangChain instrumentor.
19	     Because the provider is registered globally, the spans the reasoning_client
20	     emits for BOTH tiers — including Tier-3 (claude-agent-sdk), which LangChain
21	     instrumentation does not see — export to Phoenix too.
22	
23	run_id propagation (ADR-0015 #79): wrap a run in run_trace_context(run_id) so
24	every span produced during the run carries the run_id as session.id + metadata,
25	cross-referencing MLflow / PostgresSaver / PROV-DM.
26	"""
27	
28	from __future__ import annotations
29	
30	import atexit
31	import logging
32	import os
33	import signal
34	import sys
35	import threading
36	from contextlib import contextmanager
37	
38	# ADR-0002: traces carry SCF-derived content and must never leave the machine.
39	# The loopback guard is the single canonical validator in config.py, shared with
40	# Qdrant and MLflow (pass-5) so the data-sovereignty boundary is enforced
41	# identically everywhere; 0.0.0.0 is rejected (it binds all interfaces).
42	from ..config import LOOPBACK_HOSTS as _LOOPBACK_HOSTS  # noqa: F401  (kept for callers/tests)
43	from ..config import load_platform_config
44	from ..config import require_loopback as _require_loopback
45	
46	_log = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	# Cached registered provider — registration is idempotent within a process
49	# (repeat bootstrap calls return the same provider instead of double-registering
50	# the instrumentor, which would duplicate every span).
51	_PROVIDER = None
52	
53	
54	def _phoenix_cfg() -> dict:
55	    return load_platform_config()["observability"]["phoenix"]
56	
57	
58	def phoenix_base_url() -> str:
59	    """Return the Phoenix server base URL, e.g. http://localhost:6006."""
60	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
61	    return f"http://{cfg.get('host', 'localhost')}:{cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
62	
63	
64	def phoenix_endpoint() -> str:
65	    """Return the OTLP/HTTP traces endpoint the tracer provider exports to.
66	
67	    Validates loopback at the source (pass-5 #obs-wiring): this is public API and
68	    is called from the __main__ entrypoint and tests, not only from the already-
69	    guarded instrument_langchain(), so it must enforce its own ADR-0002 precondition."""
70	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
71	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "Phoenix OTLP export")
72	    return f"{phoenix_base_url()}/v1/traces"
73	
74	
75	def launch_server_command() -> str:
76	    """Return the shell command that starts the persistent Phoenix server.
77	
78	    Validates the bind host is loopback (#283): the server bind, not just the
79	    client export host, is what determines whether traces are reachable off-box.
80	    """
81	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
82	    host = _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "server bind")
83	    return f"phoenix serve --host {host} --port {cfg.get('port', 6006)}"
84	
85	
86	def instrument_langchain(project_name: str | None = None):
87	    """
88	    Register a global OTel tracer provider pointed at the Phoenix server (using a
89	    background BatchSpanProcessor) and enable LangChain tracing for this process.
90	
91	    Idempotent: the first call registers; subsequent calls return the cached
92	    provider without re-instrumenting (so spans are never duplicated).
93	
94	    Returns the tracer provider.
95	    """
96	    global _PROVIDER
97	    if _PROVIDER is not None:
98	        return _PROVIDER
99	
100	    from openinference.instrumentation.langchain import LangChainInstrumentor
101	    from opentelemetry import trace as _trace
102	    from phoenix.otel import register
103	
104	    cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
105	    # ADR-0002 data sovereignty: refuse to export SCF-derived traces off-box.
106	    _require_loopback(str(cfg.get("host", "localhost")), "OTLP export")
107	
108	    # Bound the OTLP/HTTP exporter so a downed Phoenix server cannot block process
109	    # teardown for the exporter's default 10s on the shutdown flush. The installed
110	    # OTel BatchProcessor.force_flush() ignores its timeout_millis argument, so the
111	    # only effective cap is at the exporter level (#277). Don't clobber an explicit
112	    # operator override.
113	    os.environ.setdefault("OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_TRACES_TIMEOUT", "2")
114	
115	    provider = register(
116	        project_name=project_name or cfg.get("project_name", "hc-grc"),
117	        endpoint=phoenix_endpoint(),
118	        batch=True,                        # BatchSpanProcessor: buffer + async export
119	        set_global_tracer_provider=True,   # so reasoning_client spans export too
120	    )
121	    # set_tracer_provider can only succeed once per process; if a competing global
122	    # provider already won the race, register() still RETURNS the Phoenix provider
123	    # (so LangChain spans, passed it explicitly, still reach Phoenix) but the GLOBAL
124	    # provider is something else — and reasoning_client's module-level tracer resolves
125	    # through the global, so Tier-3 frontier spans (their ONLY record) would silently
126	    # vanish. Surface that collision instead of dropping them silently (#278).
127	    if _trace.get_tracer_provider() is not provider:
128	        _log.warning(
129	            "Phoenix did not become the global OTel tracer provider — a competing "
130	            "provider was registered first. Tier-3 reasoning_client spans will not "
131	            "reach Phoenix. Ensure bootstrap_observability() runs before any other "
132	            "OTel instrumentation."
133	        )
134	    LangChainInstrumentor().instrument(tracer_provider=provider, skip_dep_check=True)
135	    # Flush buffered spans at process exit so the BatchSpanProcessor does not drop
136	    # the tail of a run on a clean exit / SIGINT (#215). atexit does NOT run on
137	    # SIGTERM (launchd/kill/container-stop), so also install a SIGTERM handler that
138	    # flushes then exits — otherwise the tail of a killed run is silently lost
139	    # (#281). Both flushes are bounded by the exporter timeout set above (#277).
140	    if hasattr(provider, "force_flush"):
141	        atexit.register(provider.force_flush)
142	        _install_sigterm_flush(provider)
143	    _PROVIDER = provider
144	    return provider
145	
146	
147	def _install_sigterm_flush(provider) -> None:
148	    """Flush + shut down the span processor on SIGTERM before the process exits.
149	
150	    Signal handlers can only be installed from the main thread; if we're not on it
151	    (e.g. instrumentation wired from a worker), skip silently — atexit still covers
152	    the clean-exit and SIGINT paths."""
153	    if threading.current_thread() is not threading.main_thread():
154	        return
155	
156	    def _handler(_signum, _frame):
157	        try:
158	            provider.force_flush()
159	            if hasattr(provider, "shutdown"):
160	                provider.shutdown()
161	        finally:
162	            sys.exit(0)
163	
164	    try:
165	        signal.signal(signal.SIGTERM, _handler)
166	    except (ValueError, OSError):  # pragma: no cover - non-main-thread / unsupported
167	        pass
168	
169	
170	def bootstrap_observability():
171	    """
172	    Idempotently wire tracing for the current process. Called once by each run
173	    entrypoint (graph runners). Degrades to a no-op — never raising — when tracing
174	    is disabled by config, switched off via HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING, or the
175	    instrumentation stack cannot be initialized. Returns the provider or None.
176	    """
177	    if os.environ.get("HCGRC_DISABLE_TRACING", "").strip():
178	        return None
179	    try:
180	        cfg = _phoenix_cfg()
181	    except Exception:
182	        return None
183	    if not cfg.get("enabled", True):
184	        return None
185	    # Also point MLflow at the local store so experiment tracking is actually
186	    # configured in a run path (cross-store run_id correlation, #282). Best-effort.
187	    try:
188	        from ..tracking.mlflow_setup import configure_mlflow
189	        configure_mlflow()
190	    except Exception:
191	        pass
192	    try:
193	        return instrument_langchain()
194	    except Exception:
195	        # Missing deps, server unreachable at registration, etc. — tracing is
196	        # best-effort observability and must never block the research run.
197	        return None
198	
199	
200	@contextmanager
201	def run_trace_context(run_id: str | None):
202	    """
203	    Context manager that stamps run_id onto every span produced within it, for
204	    cross-store correlation. Sets it two ways so BOTH span sources pick it up:
205	    OpenInference using_attributes (LangChain auto-spans → session.id) AND OTel
206	    baggage `hcgrc.run_id`, which the reasoning_client seam reads as a fallback so
207	    its manual T2/T3 spans carry the run_id even when the caller didn't pass it
208	    explicitly (#239). No-op if run_id is None or the helpers are unavailable.
209	    """
210	    if not run_id:
211	        yield
212	        return
213	    from contextlib import ExitStack
214	
215	    from opentelemetry import baggage, context
216	
217	    with ExitStack() as stack:
218	        try:
219	            from openinference.instrumentation import using_attributes
220	            stack.enter_context(using_attributes(session_id=run_id, metadata={"run_id": run_id}))
221	        except Exception:
222	            pass
223	        token = context.attach(baggage.set_baggage("hcgrc.run_id", run_id))
224	        try:
225	            yield
226	        finally:
227	            context.detach(token)
228	
229	
230	if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - operational entrypoint
231	    print(f"Phoenix base URL:    {phoenix_base_url()}")
232	    print(f"OTLP traces endpoint: {phoenix_endpoint()}")
233	    print(f"Start the server with: {launch_server_command()}")

1	"""
2	compute_data_split — deterministic, idempotent SCF corpus partitioning.
3	
4	Seed derivation: SHA-256 of the raw manifest file → int.from_bytes() → int seed.
5	Same manifest → identical seed → identical split on every independent run.
6	This is the idempotency guarantee tested in test_phase0/test_data_split.py.
7	
8	Split strategy: shuffle-and-slice on UNIQUE SCF control IDs (per SAP §7 / issue #60).
9	Semantically equivalent to GroupKFold when control_ids contains exactly one entry
10	per unique control. CALLER CONTRACT: control_ids must be deduplicated before calling —
11	one ID per control, not one row per STRM mapping. Passing mapping rows (280,000) instead
12	of control IDs (1,400) would produce leakage because set(train_ids) & set(test_ids) only
13	catches list-level duplicates, not the case where the same control's mapping rows land
14	in separate splits. The DataSplitAgent is responsible for deduplication before this call.
15	
16	No SCF control text appears in both train and test partitions (given the caller contract).
17	DataSplitAgent asserts len(set(train_ids) ∩ test_ids) == 0 before writing.
18	This assertion is logged and is a required Gate 1 artifact.
19	
20	Split ratios: 70% train / 15% validation / 15% test.
21	"""
22	
23	from __future__ import annotations
24	
25	import hashlib
26	from pathlib import Path
27	from typing import Any
28	
29	import numpy as np
30	
31	# ── Seed derivation ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	
33	
34	def derive_seed(manifest_path: Path | str) -> tuple[str, int]:
35	    """
36	    Derive a deterministic integer seed from the SHA-256 hash of a manifest file.
37	
38	    Returns:
39	        (manifest_hash_hex, seed_int)
40	    """
41	    path = Path(manifest_path)
42	    raw = path.read_bytes()
43	    h = hashlib.sha256(raw)
44	    manifest_hash = h.hexdigest()
45	    # 8 bytes (64-bit) → 1-in-2^64 collision probability between different manifests.
46	    # Using int.from_bytes on the raw digest (not the hex string) for portability.
47	    seed = int.from_bytes(h.digest()[:8], byteorder="big")
48	    return manifest_hash, seed
49	
50	
51	# ── Core split function ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
52	
53	
54	def compute_data_split(
55	    control_ids: list[str],
56	    manifest_path: Path | str | None = None,
57	    seed: int | None = None,
58	    train_ratio: float = 0.70,
59	    val_ratio: float = 0.15,
60	    # test_ratio is 1 - train_ratio - val_ratio = 0.15
61	) -> dict[str, Any]:
62	    """
63	    Partition control_ids into train / validation / test splits.
64	
65	    Exactly one of manifest_path or seed must be provided.
66	    - manifest_path: derive seed from SHA-256 of the file (production path)
67	    - seed: use directly (test / synthetic data path)
68	
69	    Returns:
70	        {
71	            "train_ids": [...],
72	            "val_ids": [...],
73	            "test_ids": [...],
74	            "seed": int,
75	            "manifest_hash": str | None,
76	            "n_controls": int,
77	            "split_ratios": {"train": float, "val": float, "test": float},
78	        }
79	
80	    Raises:
81	        AssertionError if any control ID appears in more than one split.
82	    """
83	    if manifest_path is not None and seed is not None:
84	        raise ValueError("Provide manifest_path OR seed, not both.")
85	    if manifest_path is None and seed is None:
86	        raise ValueError("Provide either manifest_path or seed.")
87	
88	    manifest_hash: str | None = None
89	    if manifest_path is not None:
90	        manifest_hash, seed = derive_seed(manifest_path)
91	
92	    rng = np.random.default_rng(seed)
93	    ids = np.array(control_ids)
94	    n = len(ids)
95	
96	    # Shuffle with seeded RNG
97	    shuffled_indices = rng.permutation(n)
98	    shuffled_ids = ids[shuffled_indices]
99	
100	    # Compute split boundaries
101	    train_end = int(n * train_ratio)
102	    val_end = train_end + int(n * val_ratio)
103	
104	    train_ids = shuffled_ids[:train_end].tolist()
105	    val_ids = shuffled_ids[train_end:val_end].tolist()
106	    test_ids = shuffled_ids[val_end:].tolist()
107	
108	    # ── Leakage assertion (required Gate 1 artifact) ─────────────────────────
109	    train_set = set(train_ids)
110	    val_set = set(val_ids)
111	    test_set = set(test_ids)
112	
113	    assert len(train_set & test_set) == 0, (
114	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(train_set & test_set)} control IDs appear in both train and test"
115	    )
116	    assert len(train_set & val_set) == 0, (
117	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(train_set & val_set)} control IDs appear in both train and val"
118	    )
119	    assert len(val_set & test_set) == 0, (
120	        f"LEAKAGE: {len(val_set & test_set)} control IDs appear in both val and test"
121	    )
122	    assert len(train_ids) + len(val_ids) + len(test_ids) == n, (
123	        "Split sizes do not sum to total control count"
124	    )
125	
126	    actual_train = len(train_ids) / n
127	    actual_val = len(val_ids) / n
128	    actual_test = len(test_ids) / n
129	
130	    return {
131	        "train_ids": train_ids,
132	        "val_ids": val_ids,
133	        "test_ids": test_ids,
134	        "seed": seed,
135	        "manifest_hash": manifest_hash,
136	        "n_controls": n,
137	        "split_ratios": {
138	            "train": round(actual_train, 4),
139	            "val": round(actual_val, 4),
140	            "test": round(actual_test, 4),
141	        },
142	    }
143	
144	
145	# ── LangGraph node wrapper ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146	
147	
148	def data_split_node(state: dict[str, Any], manifest_path: Path | str | None = None,
149	                    synthetic_control_ids: list[str] | None = None) -> dict[str, Any]:
150	    """
151	    LangGraph node: compute and record the data split in state.
152	
153	    In Phase 0: uses synthetic_control_ids with a fixed seed (no manifest).
154	    In Phase 1+: uses manifest_path to derive the seed from the real corpus.
155	    """
156	    # Phase 0 synthetic mode
157	    if synthetic_control_ids is not None:
158	        result = compute_data_split(synthetic_control_ids, seed=state.get("data_split_seed") or 42)
159	    elif manifest_path is not None:
160	        result = compute_data_split(list(state.get("control_ids", [])), manifest_path=manifest_path)
161	    else:
162	        raise ValueError("Provide manifest_path or synthetic_control_ids.")
163	
164	    return {
165	        "data_split_manifest_hash": result["manifest_hash"],
166	        "data_split_seed": result["seed"],
167	        "data_split_verified": True,
168	        "prov_activities": [

def _merge_artifacts(a: list[str], b: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    """Append b onto a, dropping any value already present in a OR earlier in b.
    Order-preserving, O(n+m). The previous `x not in set(a)` deduped against a but
    not within b, so a single agent returning a duplicate path slipped through —
    violating the 'deduped on merge' contract (pass-6)."""
    seen = set(a)
    out = list(a)
    for x in b:
        if x not in seen:
            seen.add(x)
            out.append(x)
--
def _merge_hypotheses(a: list[dict[str, Any]], b: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Append b onto a, deduping by hypothesis 'id' (latest wins, position kept).

    Entries without an 'id' are kept as-is. Prevents a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize
    loop from accumulating duplicate hypotheses into the pre-registered set."""
    merged = list(a)
    index = {h["id"]: i for i, h in enumerate(merged) if isinstance(h, dict) and h.get("id")}
    for h in b:
        hid = h.get("id") if isinstance(h, dict) else None
        if hid is not None and hid in index:
            merged[index[hid]] = h
--
    # Merge reducer: {**a, **b} ensures concurrent gate writes are safe by
    # construction. gate_coordinator_node is the canonical writer, but the
    # reducer protects against any future path that returns a partial update.
    # Fixes: ADR-0015 §75 serial guarantee (F1) + silent overwrite risk (F4).
    gate_status: Annotated[dict[GateID, GateStatus], lambda a, b: {**a, **b}]

    # ── Failure events (written by gate nodes on rejection) ───────────────────
    # Agent Evolution monitors this store for routing re-optimization signals.
    failure_events: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]

    # ── Research artifacts (append-only) ──────────────────────────────────────
    hypotheses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]
    findings: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]

    # ── Data split record ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    # Un-reduced (last-write-wins) scalars. Written by exactly one node per phase —
    # data_split_node in Phase 0, DataStewardAgent in Phase 1 — never concurrently,
    # so last-write-wins is safe by sequencing, not by a reducer (#212). Immutable
    # after Gate 1. If these ever become concurrently written, add a reducer.
    data_split_manifest_hash: str | None  # SHA-256 of the raw manifest file
    data_split_seed: int | None  # derived from manifest hash (int.from_bytes)
    data_split_verified: bool  # True after idempotency assertion passes

    # ── Phase 1 execution tracking ────────────────────────────────────────────
    # phase_1_ready: True after data pipeline writes validated splits to disk.
    # exploratory_complete: True after all P1-P5 nodes complete (or graceful stub).
    phase_1_ready: bool
    exploratory_complete: bool

--
    eda_artifacts: Annotated[list[str], _merge_artifacts]
    eda_agent_statuses: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]

    # ── Formalized hypothesis set (append, deduped by id) ─────────────────────
    # Written by HypothesisFormalizerAgent before Gate 2.
    # Each entry is a FormalHypothesis dict (see src/agents/hypothesis_formalizer).
    # Deduped by hypothesis id (latest wins) so a Gate-2 reject → re-formalize loop
    # cannot accumulate duplicate hypotheses into the set Gate 2 locks (pass-4 #2).
    hypothesis_set: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: _merge_hypotheses(a, b)]

    # ── Orchestrator run-start grounding ──────────────────────────────────────
    # Produced by t00_orchestrator_node via the reasoning_client (Tier 2). The
    # orchestrator reasons about the run *inside the graph* rather than the work
    # being done by hand outside the framework (ADR-0016 — the agency contract).
    # None when the reasoning backend was unavailable (graceful degradation).
    run_grounding: str | None

    # ── Escalation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    escalation_issue_number: int | None  # GitHub issue number if parked
--
    messages: Annotated[list, add_messages]

    # ── Provenance ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    prov_activities: Annotated[list[dict[str, Any]], lambda a, b: a + b]

def route_after_gate_2(state: HCGRCState) -> str:
    """Conditional edge after Gate 2."""
    gate_status = state.get("gate_status", {})
    gate_2 = gate_status.get("gate_2")

    if gate_2 is None:
        return "end"

    decision = gate_2.get("decision", "rejected")

    if decision == "approved":
        return "confirmatory_entry"

        return "end"

I conducted a comprehensive audit of the lg-graph dimension (LangGraph layer) in the HC-GRC hardening pass 7 codebase. The audit focused on:
